sentences.en: The squares are set into the path between the drive and the pool, each one wide enough to stand on, purple pieces ranked against green, and on a sunny Saturday in March I walked between the pawns and found Guo under the pergola, deep in an argument with Bella Garcia-Camargo about a founder.
sentences.zh: 这些方块被设置在车道和游泳池之间的小路上，每个方块都足够宽，可以站在上面，紫色的棋子与绿色的棋子相对排列，三月一个阳光明媚的星期六，我在棋子之间行走，发现郭在凉棚下，正在与贝拉·加西亚-卡马戈就创始人的问题进行深入的争论。

blocks.html_en: Sparring with Guo is normal, and Garcia-Camargo, an investor at Conviction, had learned this before she took the job. She had rowed at Stanford and for the U.S. national team, then spent time at Bridgewater. When Conviction came calling she was weighing an offer from OpenAI to work as an application engineer. Guo’s counsel, as Garcia-Camargo remembers it, was not a pitch for Conviction but a dare. “If you’re going to do something else,” Guo told her, “just make it the most aggressive thing that you could possibly be doing. I’m happy to call Kevin and we’ll find you a better job. But that [job] is not aggressive enough for you.” Kevin Weil was then OpenAI’s chief product officer.

sentences.en: Sparring with Guo is normal, and Garcia-Camargo, an investor at Conviction, had learned this before she took the job.
sentences.zh: 与郭发生争执是很正常的，Conviction 的投资者加西亚-卡马戈在接受这份工作之前就已经了解到这一点。

sentences.en: She had rowed at Stanford and for the U.S. national team, then spent time at Bridgewater.
sentences.zh: 她曾在斯坦福大学划船并为美国国家队效力，然后在布里奇沃特度过了一段时光。

sentences.en: When Conviction came calling she was weighing an offer from OpenAI to work as an application engineer.
sentences.zh: 当 Conviction 打电话过来时，她正在权衡 OpenAI 提供的应用工程师职位。

sentences.en: Guo’s counsel, as Garcia-Camargo remembers it, was not a pitch for Conviction but a dare.
sentences.zh: 加西亚-卡马戈记得，郭的建议并不是宣扬定罪，而是一次挑战。

sentences.en: “If you’re going to do something else,” Guo told her, “just make it the most aggressive thing that you could possibly be doing.
sentences.zh: “如果你想做别的事情，”郭告诉她，“就让它成为你可能做的最激进的事情。

sentences.en: I’m happy to call Kevin and we’ll find you a better job.
sentences.zh: 我很高兴给凯文打电话，我们将为您找到一份更好的工作。

sentences.en: But that [job] is not aggressive enough for you.”
sentences.zh: 但那份[工作]对你来说还不够激进。”

sentences.en: Kevin Weil was then OpenAI’s chief product officer.
sentences.zh: Kevin Weil 当时是 OpenAI 的首席产品官。

blocks.html_en: While Guo and Garcia-Camargo were deep in it, the property behind them had filled with founders. Thirty-five in all, across 14 companies. Conviction had flown them in from Vancouver and Tel Aviv and London and Tallinn and parceled them out among seven Airbnbs across San Francisco. They had passed through OpenAI, Scale, Ramp, Kalshi, MIT, and Anduril; one had served as chief of staff to Ken Griffin. The youngest had turned 18 the day before. He had been ranked among the top five programmers in Estonia before dropping out of high school. His employers expected him to spend $2.1 million on Claude this year. They had given him a faster model, Opus 4.6, for his birthday.

sentences.en: While Guo and Garcia-Camargo were deep in it, the property behind them had filled with founders.
sentences.zh: 当郭和加西亚-卡马戈深陷其中时，他们背后的财产却充满了创始人。

sentences.en: Thirty-five in all, across 14 companies.
sentences.zh: 总共 35 家，分布在 14 家公司。

sentences.en: Conviction had flown them in from Vancouver and Tel Aviv and London and Tallinn and parceled them out among seven Airbnbs across San Francisco.
sentences.zh: 定罪将他们从温哥华、特拉维夫、伦敦和塔林空运过来，然后将他们分配到旧金山各地的七个 Airbnb 房源中。

sentences.en: They had passed through OpenAI, Scale, Ramp, Kalshi, MIT, and Anduril; one had served as chief of staff to Ken Griffin.
sentences.zh: 他们先后通过了OpenAI、Scale、Ramp、Kalshi、MIT、Anduril；其中一位曾担任肯·格里芬的幕僚长。

sentences.en: The youngest had turned 18 the day before.
sentences.zh: 最小的孩子前一天刚满 18 岁。

sentences.en: He had been ranked among the top five programmers in Estonia before dropping out of high school.
sentences.zh: 在高中辍学之前，他已跻身爱沙尼亚前五名程序员之列。

sentences.en: His employers expected him to spend $2.1 million on Claude this year.
sentences.zh: 他的雇主预计他今年会在克劳德身上花费 210 万美元。

sentences.en: They had given him a faster model, Opus 4.6, for his birthday.
sentences.zh: 他们送给他一个更快的型号 Opus 4.6 作为他的生日礼物。

blocks.html_en: None of this was apparent from the poolside, where the scene looked like a WeWork summer camp. A few shot baskets on the tennis court. Others rode a zipline down through the trees. Some sank into the sofas by the waterfall. At the far edge of the garden, the 18-year-old, wearing a black baseball cap a few sizes too big—across the front, in lowercase, it read agents—jumped on a trampoline. The rest sat near Guo at the two long tables under the pergola, heads bowed over their MacBooks, building software to place outbound sales calls, conduct market research, shoot films, design virtual worlds, manage the concierge needs of pets, and wage “agentic warfare.”

sentences.en: None of this was apparent from the poolside, where the scene looked like a WeWork summer camp.
sentences.zh: 从泳池边来看，这一切都看不出来，那里的场景看起来就像是 WeWork 夏令营。

sentences.en: A few shot baskets on the tennis court.
sentences.zh: 网球场上有几个投篮。

sentences.en: Others rode a zipline down through the trees.
sentences.zh: 其他人则乘坐滑索穿过树林。

sentences.en: Some sank into the sofas by the waterfall.
sentences.zh: 有些人陷进了瀑布边的沙发里。

sentences.en: At the far edge of the garden, the 18-year-old, wearing a black baseball cap a few sizes too big—across the front, in lowercase, it read agents—jumped on a trampoline.
sentences.zh: 在花园的远处边缘，这位 18 岁的年轻人戴着一顶大了几号的黑色棒球帽——正面的小写字母写着“特工”——在蹦床上跳了起来。

sentences.en: The rest sat near Guo at the two long tables under the pergola, heads bowed over their MacBooks, building software to place outbound sales calls, conduct market research, shoot films, design virtual worlds, manage the concierge needs of pets, and wage “agentic warfare.”
sentences.zh: 其余的人坐在郭文贵身边，坐在凉棚下的两张长桌旁，低着头，低着 MacBook，开发软件来拨打外线销售电话、进行市场研究、拍摄电影、设计虚拟世界、管理宠物的礼宾需求，以及发动“代理战”。

blocks.html_en: This was Embed, an accelerator—though don’t call it an accelerator—that Conviction has run twice a year since 2023. Out of a thousand or so applications, they choose around a dozen teams, write a small $250,000 check, and treat them as portfolio companies for 10 weeks. Each cycle opens with a weekend like the one I witnessed, where the founders are inducted into the Conviction way. Among the speakers that weekend were Bret Taylor, Mike Krieger, Parker Conrad, and Andrej Karpathy. The program closes with a demo day in front of 100 venture capitalists.

sentences.en: This was Embed, an accelerator—though don’t call it an accelerator—that Conviction has run twice a year since 2023.
sentences.zh: 这就是 Embed，一个加速器（尽管不叫它加速器），Conviction 自 2023 年以来每年运行两次。

sentences.en: Out of a thousand or so applications, they choose around a dozen teams, write a small $250,000 check, and treat them as portfolio companies for 10 weeks.
sentences.zh: 他们从大约 1000 份申请中选择了大约 12 个团队，开出一张 25 万美元的小额支票，并将它们视为投资组合公司 10 周。

sentences.en: Each cycle opens with a weekend like the one I witnessed, where the founders are inducted into the Conviction way.
sentences.zh: 每个周期都以一个周末开始，就像我亲眼所见的那样，创始人们被引入了信念之路。

sentences.en: Among the speakers that weekend were Bret Taylor, Mike Krieger, Parker Conrad, and Andrej Karpathy.
sentences.zh: 那个周末的演讲者包括 Bret Taylor、Mike Krieger、Parker Conrad 和 Andrej Karpathy。

sentences.en: The program closes with a demo day in front of 100 venture capitalists.
sentences.zh: 该项目以在 100 名风险投资家面前进行的演示日结束。

blocks.html_en: “I hate the word accelerator,” said Pranav Reddy, Guo’s earliest hire at Conviction. “What very smart 21-year-old is excited to be ‘accelerated’?” Conviction hopes to make money from Embed, but that is not really the point. Guo’s explanation for the exercise is simple. She wants to know what is happening, all the time, and the surest way to know is to meet the youngest companies pushing at the edge of technology. It is also, twice a year, a levy. The world’s most promising young founders are brought together, sat in front of the Taylors and the Karpathys, then sent back out to build in the shadow of the machine.

sentences.en: “I hate the word accelerator,” said Pranav Reddy, Guo’s earliest hire at Conviction.
sentences.zh: “我讨厌加速器这个词，”郭在 Conviction 最早聘用的普拉纳夫·雷迪 (Pranav Reddy) 说。

sentences.en: “What very smart 21-year-old is excited to be ‘accelerated’?”
sentences.zh: “哪个非常聪明的 21 岁年轻人会对‘加速’感到兴奋？”

sentences.en: Conviction hopes to make money from Embed, but that is not really the point.
sentences.zh: Conviction 希望从 Embed 赚钱，但这并不是真正的重点。

sentences.en: Guo’s explanation for the exercise is simple.
sentences.zh: 郭对这项练习的解释很简单。

sentences.en: She wants to know what is happening, all the time, and the surest way to know is to meet the youngest companies pushing at the edge of technology.
sentences.zh: 她一直想知道正在发生什么，而最可靠的了解方法就是会见推动技术前沿的最年轻的公司。

sentences.en: It is also, twice a year, a levy.
sentences.zh: 这也是每年两次的征税。

sentences.en: The world’s most promising young founders are brought together, sat in front of the Taylors and the Karpathys, then sent back out to build in the shadow of the machine.
sentences.zh: 世界上最有前途的年轻创始人聚集在一起，坐在泰勒家族和卡帕蒂家族前面，然后被送回机器的阴影下进行建设。

blocks.html_en: Her week had begun in a less pastoral setting. Five days earlier she had been in San Jose, in a pale purple blazer with a small American flag pinned to the lapel and a broadcaster’s headset over her ears, hosting the three-hour pregame to Jensen Huang’s keynote at Nvidia’s annual developer conference. Nvidia is the most valuable company on earth, worth $5 trillion. The chips it designs are what the boom is made of. Some 15,000 people were already filing into the conference hall, watching Guo, waiting for Huang.

sentences.en: Her week had begun in a less pastoral setting.
sentences.zh: 她的一周是在不太田园风光的环境中开始的。

sentences.en: Five days earlier she had been in San Jose, in a pale purple blazer with a small American flag pinned to the lapel and a broadcaster’s headset over her ears, hosting the three-hour pregame to Jensen Huang’s keynote at Nvidia’s annual developer conference.
sentences.zh: 五天前，她在圣何塞主持了英伟达年度开发者大会上黄仁勋主题演讲的三小时赛前赛，她身穿淡紫色西装外套，翻领上别着一面小美国国旗，耳朵上戴着广播耳机。

sentences.en: Nvidia is the most valuable company on earth, worth $5 trillion.
sentences.zh: Nvidia 是地球上最有价值的公司，价值 5 万亿美元。

sentences.en: The chips it designs are what the boom is made of.
sentences.zh: 它设计的芯片就是繁荣的组成部分。

sentences.en: Some 15,000 people were already filing into the conference hall, watching Guo, waiting for Huang.
sentences.zh: 大约 15,000 人已经鱼贯进入会议厅，看着郭，等待着黄。

blocks.html_en: Her co-hosts were Gavin Baker, whose firm Atreides had been an early institutional investor in Nvidia, and Alfred Lin of Sequoia, which had been Nvidia’s first venture backer. Lin’s co-managing partner at Sequoia is Guo’s husband, Pat Grady. Two hours in, Huang walked onto the set in his black leather jacket. “Jensen,” Guo said, “welcome to your own party.”

sentences.en: Her co-hosts were Gavin Baker, whose firm Atreides had been an early institutional investor in Nvidia, and Alfred Lin of Sequoia, which had been Nvidia’s first venture backer.
sentences.zh: 她的共同主持人是加文·贝克 (Gavin Baker) 和红杉资本的阿尔弗雷德·林 (Alfred Lin)，前者的 Atreides 公司是英伟达的早期机构投资者，后者是英伟达的第一个风险投资者。

sentences.en: Lin’s co-managing partner at Sequoia is Guo’s husband, Pat Grady.
sentences.zh: 林在红杉资本的联合管理合伙人是郭的丈夫帕特·格雷迪。

sentences.en: Two hours in, Huang walked onto the set in his black leather jacket.
sentences.zh: 两个小时后，黄穿着黑色皮夹克走进片场。

sentences.en: “Jensen,” Guo said, “welcome to your own party.”
sentences.zh: “詹森，”郭说，“欢迎来到你自己的聚会。”

blocks.html_en: Huang turned to the man wearing red lobster claws on his head. Peter Steinberger had brought agents to the masses. Working alone in his Vienna apartment, he had built OpenClaw, an open-source tool that put an agent in your phone that you could WhatsApp like a friend. Within two weeks of release, the project had crossed 100,000 stars on GitHub. By spring, it was the most popular open-source project the site had ever seen. The agent that had ordered Friedman to drink a bottle of water and rerouted his Tesla to Whole Foods was a claw. In the thick of the frenzy, in February, OpenAI had hired its maker.

sentences.en: Huang turned to the man wearing red lobster claws on his head.
sentences.zh: 黄转向那个头上戴着红色龙虾爪的男人。

sentences.en: Peter Steinberger had brought agents to the masses.
sentences.zh: 彼得·斯坦伯格将特工带到了大众面前。

sentences.en: Working alone in his Vienna apartment, he had built OpenClaw, an open-source tool that put an agent in your phone that you could WhatsApp like a friend.
sentences.zh: 他在维也纳的公寓里独自工作，开发了 OpenClaw，这是一个开源工具，可以在你的手机中放置一个代理，你可以像朋友一样使用 WhatsApp。

sentences.en: Within two weeks of release, the project had crossed 100,000 stars on GitHub.
sentences.zh: 发布两周内，该项目在 GitHub 上的 Star 数就突破了 10 万。

sentences.en: By spring, it was the most popular open-source project the site had ever seen.
sentences.zh: 到了春季，它已成为该网站有史以来最受欢迎的开源项目。

sentences.en: The agent that had ordered Friedman to drink a bottle of water and rerouted his Tesla to Whole Foods was a claw.
sentences.zh: 命令弗里德曼喝一瓶水并将他的特斯拉改道到全食超市的特工是一只爪子。

sentences.en: In the thick of the frenzy, in February, OpenAI had hired its maker.
sentences.zh: 在二月份最疯狂的时候，OpenAI 聘请了它的制造者。

blocks.html_en: Huang shook Steinberger’s hand and asked whether the Nvidia team had been working with him. “We were cooking last night,” Steinberger said. Huang nodded. “Somehow,” he said, “the faster and the smarter the claws are, the harder we work. Just like the internet made us all busier, AI is going to make us busier.”

sentences.en: Huang shook Steinberger’s hand and asked whether the Nvidia team had been working with him.
sentences.zh: 黄握了斯坦伯格的手，询问英伟达团队是否一直在与他合作。

sentences.en: “We were cooking last night,” Steinberger said.
sentences.zh: “我们昨晚正在做饭，”斯坦伯格说。

sentences.en: Huang nodded.
sentences.zh: 黄点点头。

sentences.en: “Somehow,” he said, “the faster and the smarter the claws are, the harder we work.
sentences.zh: “不知何故，”他说，“爪子越快、越聪明，我们就越努力。

sentences.en: Just like the internet made us all busier, AI is going to make us busier.”
sentences.zh: 就像互联网让我们更加忙碌一样，人工智能也会让我们更加忙碌。”

blocks.html_en: I first met Guo and her team the day before, on Friday, at Conviction’s office in the Mission District of San Francisco. It was the opening day of Embed. The office is not what it appears to be, which is a gym, or the apartment of a wealthy and easily distracted graduate student. It is one large open space with a stone floor and in the middle of the room, on its own stand, with a bowl of chalk beneath it, is a steel pull-up bar. It is for dead hangs. You chalk your hands, grip the bar, lift your feet off the floor, and hang until your arms give out. When someone steps up to it, the team blasts “Eye of the Tiger,” loud, and starts a timer.

sentences.en: I first met Guo and her team the day before, on Friday, at Conviction’s office in the Mission District of San Francisco.
sentences.zh: 我第一次见到郭和她的团队是在前一天的星期五，地点是旧金山教会区的 Conviction 办公室。

sentences.en: It was the opening day of Embed.
sentences.zh: 这一天是 Embed 的开幕日。

sentences.en: The office is not what it appears to be, which is a gym, or the apartment of a wealthy and easily distracted graduate student.
sentences.zh: 办公室并不像看上去的那样，它是一个健身房，或者是一个富有且容易分心的研究生的公寓。

sentences.en: It is one large open space with a stone floor and in the middle of the room, on its own stand, with a bowl of chalk beneath it, is a steel pull-up bar.
sentences.zh: 这是一个很大的开放空间，铺着石头地板，在房间的中间，有一个钢制引体向上杆，在它自己的架子上，下面放着一碗粉笔。

sentences.en: It is for dead hangs.
sentences.zh: 这是为了死挂。

sentences.en: You chalk your hands, grip the bar, lift your feet off the floor, and hang until your arms give out.
sentences.zh: 你用粉笔写下双手，抓住杠铃，将脚抬离地板，然后悬挂起来，直到手臂无力。

sentences.en: When someone steps up to it, the team blasts “Eye of the Tiger,” loud, and starts a timer.
sentences.zh: 当有人靠近时，团队会大声播放“老虎之眼”，并启动计时器。

blocks.html_en: Just inside the door stands a bookshelf, on top of which sits a crystal hammer and an outsized purple pill, and beyond it sit two reclining Eames chairs and a grey sofa scattered with purple cushions. Strewn around are a small green chessboard, a few drones, two pickleball paddles bearing the name of a bank that no longer exists, and an M64, Palmer Luckey’s remake of the Nintendo 64. On the far wall, near the kitchen, a black flag reads, WE DO THIS NOT BECAUSE IT IS EASY, BUT BECAUSE WE THOUGHT IT WOULD BE EASY. To the right, past a 90-inch television, a robot dog lies folded onto its joints beneath a whiteboard that reads NO SMALL IDEAS. Two neon signs glow all day, one spelling out CONVICTION, the other tracing the firm’s logo.

sentences.en: Just inside the door stands a bookshelf, on top of which sits a crystal hammer and an outsized purple pill, and beyond it sit two reclining Eames chairs and a grey sofa scattered with purple cushions.
sentences.zh: 门里面有一个书架，上面放着一把水晶锤和一颗超大的紫色药丸，书架后面放着两张伊姆斯躺椅和一张灰色沙发，上面散落着紫色的靠垫。

sentences.en: Strewn around are a small green chessboard, a few drones, two pickleball paddles bearing the name of a bank that no longer exists, and an M64, Palmer Luckey’s remake of the Nintendo 64.
sentences.zh: 周围散落着一个绿色的小棋盘、几架无人机、两个带有银行名称的泡菜球桨，该银行已不复存在，还有一台 M64，Palmer Luckey 对 Nintendo 64 的翻版。

sentences.en: On the far wall, near the kitchen, a black flag reads, WE DO THIS NOT BECAUSE IT IS EASY, BUT BECAUSE WE THOUGHT IT WOULD BE EASY.
sentences.zh: 在厨房附近的远处墙上，一面黑色旗帜上写着：我们这样做不是因为这很容易，而是因为我们认为这会很容易。

sentences.en: To the right, past a 90-inch television, a robot dog lies folded onto its joints beneath a whiteboard that reads NO SMALL IDEAS.
sentences.zh: 右边，经过一台 90 英寸的电视机，一只机器狗折叠起来，躺在白板上，上面写着“没有小想法”。

sentences.en: Two neon signs glow all day, one spelling out CONVICTION, the other tracing the firm’s logo.
sentences.zh: 两个霓虹灯招牌一整天都在发光，一个写着“CONVICTION”（信念），另一个则描绘着公司的标志。

blocks.html_en: There are 20 desks and monitors, enough to seat a startup, and in a sense it does. Conviction is eight people, four of whom invest; the spare desks are loaned to founders and portfolio companies who need somewhere to work. The rules are about leaving rather than arriving. “If you hire eight people, or you raise more than $20 million,” Guo said, “it’s time.” Most tenants get the message. Alex Graveley, the first architect of GitHub Copilot, camped here while he built his company. Niki Parmar and Ashish Vaswani, two of the authors of the paper that introduced the Transformer, and with it more or less the present era, took a row of eight desks. Karpathy worked from these desks before he moved his family south, at which point he migrated to Conviction’s new outpost in Palo Alto.

sentences.en: There are 20 desks and monitors, enough to seat a startup, and in a sense it does.
sentences.zh: 这里有 20 张桌子和显示器，足以容纳一家初创公司，从某种意义上来说也确实如此。

sentences.en: Conviction is eight people, four of whom invest; the spare desks are loaned to founders and portfolio companies who need somewhere to work.
sentences.zh: 定罪是八个人，其中四人投资；备用办公桌借给需要工作场所的创始人和投资组合公司。

sentences.en: The rules are about leaving rather than arriving.
sentences.zh: 规则是关于离开而不是到达。

sentences.en: “If you hire eight people, or you raise more than $20 million,” Guo said, “it’s time.”
sentences.zh: “如果你雇佣了八个人，或者你筹集了超过 2000 万美元，”郭说，“就是时候了。”

sentences.en: Most tenants get the message.
sentences.zh: 大多数租户都明白这一点。

sentences.en: Alex Graveley, the first architect of GitHub Copilot, camped here while he built his company.
sentences.zh: Alex Graveley 是 GitHub Copilot 的第一位架构师，他在创建自己的公司时曾在这里扎营。

sentences.en: Niki Parmar and Ashish Vaswani, two of the authors of the paper that introduced the Transformer, and with it more or less the present era, took a row of eight desks.
sentences.zh: 尼基·帕尔玛 (Niki Parmar) 和阿什什·瓦斯瓦尼 (Ashish Vaswani) 是介绍变形金刚及其或多或少的当今时代论文的两位作者，他们占据了一排八张桌子。

sentences.en: Karpathy worked from these desks before he moved his family south, at which point he migrated to Conviction’s new outpost in Palo Alto.
sentences.zh: 卡帕蒂 (Karpathy) 在举家南迁之前就在这些办公桌上工作，随后他又搬到了 Conviction 在帕洛阿尔托的新前哨基地。

blocks.html_en: The scruffiness is deliberate, which you can suppose by considering the woman who built the place. Guo is refined. She dresses, as Garcia-Camargo noted, like she’s in New York. Most successful venture firms aim to hypnotize founders by sitting them down for an hour amid the discreet art, the modernist seating, the floor-to-ceiling glass looking out on the bay, the assistant gliding in with the still water and the sparkling, the whole hushed choreography of capital, the implication being that all of this, yes, all this, could be yours, if only you would take our money. Conviction implies nothing of the sort. Guo’s house down the peninsula has the Sequoia look about it, though that, she’ll tell you, was Grady’s doing. The office is hers.

sentences.en: The scruffiness is deliberate, which you can suppose by considering the woman who built the place.
sentences.zh: 这种邋遢是故意的，你可以通过考虑建造这个地方的女人来推测这一点。

sentences.en: Guo is refined.
sentences.zh: 郭是精致的。

sentences.en: She dresses, as Garcia-Camargo noted, like she’s in New York.
sentences.zh: 正如加西亚-卡马戈所说，她的穿着就像在纽约一样。

sentences.en: Most successful venture firms aim to hypnotize founders by sitting them down for an hour amid the discreet art, the modernist seating, the floor-to-ceiling glass looking out on the bay, the assistant gliding in with the still water and the sparkling, the whole hushed choreography of capital, the implication being that all of this, yes, all this, could be yours, if only you would take our money.
sentences.zh: 大多数成功的风险投资公司的目标是让创始人在低调的艺术、现代主义的座椅、俯瞰海湾的落地玻璃、在平静的水面和波光粼粼的背景下滑行的助理一个小时，来催眠创始人，这一切都意味着，所有这一切，是的，所有这一切，都可能是你的，只要你愿意接受我们的钱。

sentences.en: Conviction implies nothing of the sort.
sentences.zh: 定罪并不意味着任何此类内容。

sentences.en: Guo’s house down the peninsula has the Sequoia look about it, though that, she’ll tell you, was Grady’s doing.
sentences.zh: 郭在半岛上的房子有红杉的外观，尽管她会告诉你，那是格雷迪做的。

sentences.en: The office is hers.
sentences.zh: 办公室是她的。

blocks.html_en: It’s on York Street. A few blocks west is the Pioneer Building, OpenAI’s first headquarters; Elon Musk holds the lease, and after OpenAI moved out he installed xAI in its place. A short walk north is Mira Murati’s Thinking Machines, the lab she founded after leaving OpenAI as chief technology officer. Conviction backed it. The blocks between are thick with young AI companies. Someone began calling the neighborhood the arena, and the name stuck.

sentences.en: It’s on York Street.
sentences.zh: 它位于约克街。

sentences.en: A few blocks west is the Pioneer Building, OpenAI’s first headquarters; Elon Musk holds the lease, and after OpenAI moved out he installed xAI in its place.
sentences.zh: 向西几个街区就是先锋大厦，OpenAI 的第一个总部；埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 持有该租约，在 OpenAI 搬出后，他安装了 xAI。

sentences.en: A short walk north is Mira Murati’s Thinking Machines, the lab she founded after leaving OpenAI as chief technology officer.
sentences.zh: 向北走一小段路就是 Mira Murati 的 Thinking Machines 实验室，这是她在辞去 OpenAI 首席技术官职务后创立的实验室。

sentences.en: Conviction backed it.
sentences.zh: 信念支持了它。

sentences.en: The blocks between are thick with young AI companies.
sentences.zh: 中间的街区挤满了年轻的人工智能公司。

sentences.en: Someone began calling the neighborhood the arena, and the name stuck.
sentences.zh: 有人开始把这个街区称为竞技场，这个名字就这样流传了下来。

blocks.html_en: Guo could have set up shop in Palo Alto, eight minutes from her house, eight minutes from Greylock where she used to work. So could Vernal, who lives down the peninsula too. On a good run, it is a 40-minute drive to work. “I tell Mike,” Guo said, “that we locked ourselves in jail. But when we decided that AI was going to be like the Industrial Revolution, you don’t observe that from afar.” The whole setup is built to attract a particular kind of person: the worker ant leaving the biggest colonies behind.

sentences.en: Guo could have set up shop in Palo Alto, eight minutes from her house, eight minutes from Greylock where she used to work.
sentences.zh: 郭本可以在帕洛阿尔托开店，距离她家八分钟路程，距离她以前工作的格雷洛克八分钟路程。

sentences.en: So could Vernal, who lives down the peninsula too.
sentences.zh: 弗纳尔也可以，他也住在半岛上。

sentences.en: On a good run, it is a 40-minute drive to work.
sentences.zh: 如果运行良好，开车 40 分钟即可到达上班地点。

sentences.en: “I tell Mike,” Guo said, “that we locked ourselves in jail.
sentences.zh: “我告诉迈克，”郭说，“我们把自己锁在监狱里。

sentences.en: But when we decided that AI was going to be like the Industrial Revolution, you don’t observe that from afar.”
sentences.zh: 但当我们决定人工智能将像工业革命一样时，你无法从远处观察到这一点。”

sentences.en: The whole setup is built to attract a particular kind of person: the worker ant leaving the biggest colonies behind.
sentences.zh: 整个设置是为了吸引特定类型的人：工蚁留下最大的蚁群。

blocks.html_en: When I put the term to her, she disagreed forcefully. “I don’t see founders as worker ants at all,” she said. “Ants don’t start rebellions.”

sentences.en: When I put the term to her, she disagreed forcefully.
sentences.zh: 当我向她提出这个词时，她强烈反对。

sentences.en: “I don’t see founders as worker ants at all,” she said.
sentences.zh: “我根本不认为创始人是工蚁，”她说。

sentences.en: “Ants don’t start rebellions.”
sentences.zh: “蚂蚁不会发动叛乱。”

blocks.html_en: From the beginning, Conviction made a commitment to the research community in a way that was, at the time, somewhat unusual. The worker ant was not, in 2022, an obvious venture bet. Researchers published; founders shipped. “I was actually uncommitted that we should back a lot of researchers,” she said, “even though we have a good number in the portfolio now.” What she had instead was a curiosity about what was happening on the technical frontier, and a nose for who in any given field was worth knowing. She and Reddy traced the most interesting ideas back to the people behind them, and tried to develop a view on which fundamental ideas would matter.

sentences.en: From the beginning, Conviction made a commitment to the research community in a way that was, at the time, somewhat unusual.
sentences.zh: 从一开始，Conviction 就以一种在当时有些不寻常的方式对研究界做出了承诺。

sentences.en: The worker ant was not, in 2022, an obvious venture bet.
sentences.zh: 到 2022 年，工蚁显然还不是一个明显的风险投资对象。

sentences.en: Researchers published; founders shipped.
sentences.zh: 研究人员发表；创始人发货。

sentences.en: “I was actually uncommitted that we should back a lot of researchers,” she said, “even though we have a good number in the portfolio now.”
sentences.zh: “我实际上并没有承诺我们应该支持很多研究人员，”她说，“尽管我们现在的投资组合中有很多研究人员。”

sentences.en: What she had instead was a curiosity about what was happening on the technical frontier, and a nose for who in any given field was worth knowing.
sentences.zh: 相反，她对技术前沿正在发生的事情充满好奇，并且对任何特定领域中谁值得了解。

sentences.en: She and Reddy traced the most interesting ideas back to the people behind them, and tried to develop a view on which fundamental ideas would matter.
sentences.zh: 她和雷迪将最有趣的想法追溯到其背后的人，并试图形成一种观点，认为哪些基本想法很重要。

blocks.html_en: One of Conviction’s early investments was a Series A check into Mistral, Europe’s leading AI lab, co-founded by Arthur Mensch, a worker ant out of DeepMind who had contributed to the early work on retrieval augmentation. “Mistral wasn’t even a technical belief,” she said. “It was a belief that people with a more academic profile who can do this science can do amazing things that are non-obvious.” The company is now reportedly raising at a $23 billion valuation.

sentences.en: One of Conviction’s early investments was a Series A check into Mistral, Europe’s leading AI lab, co-founded by Arthur Mensch, a worker ant out of DeepMind who had contributed to the early work on retrieval augmentation.
sentences.zh: Conviction 的早期投资之一是对欧洲领先的人工智能实验室 Mistral 的 A 轮融资，该实验室由来自 DeepMind 的工蚁 Arthur Mensch 联合创立，他为检索增强的早期工作做出了贡献。

sentences.en: “Mistral wasn’t even a technical belief,” she said.
sentences.zh: “米斯特拉尔甚至不是一个技术信仰，”她说。

sentences.en: “It was a belief that people with a more academic profile who can do this science can do amazing things that are non-obvious.”
sentences.zh: “人们相信，具有更多学术背景、能够从事这门科学的人可以做出一些不明显的惊人事情。”

sentences.en: The company is now reportedly raising at a $23 billion valuation.
sentences.zh: 据报道，该公司目前的估值为 230 亿美元。

blocks.html_en: “One of the reasons to start Conviction,” Guo told me, “was that not all of your priors make sense anymore.” Six months into Conviction, she launched a podcast with Elad Gil that speaks to AI and tech leaders around the same observation, and named it No Priors. “It’s an in-joke for machine learning,” she said, “but we mean it.” On the Friday I was there, she released a podcast with Karpathy. It’s been watched nearly a million times. In the last six weeks, Guo and Gil have spoken with Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella, Meta founder Mark Zuckerberg, and Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan.

sentences.en: “One of the reasons to start Conviction,” Guo told me, “was that not all of your priors make sense anymore.”
sentences.zh: “开始定罪的原因之一，”郭告诉我，“是你的先验不再有意义了。”

sentences.en: Six months into Conviction, she launched a podcast with Elad Gil that speaks to AI and tech leaders around the same observation, and named it No Priors.
sentences.zh: 《定罪》六个月后，她与埃拉德·吉尔 (Elad Gil) 一起推出了一个播客，围绕相同的观察结果与人工智能和技术领导者进行对话，并将其命名为“No Priors”。

sentences.en: “It’s an in-joke for machine learning,” she said, “but we mean it.”
sentences.zh: “这是机器学习的一个笑话，”她说，“但我们是认真的。”

sentences.en: On the Friday I was there, she released a podcast with Karpathy.
sentences.zh: 我在那里的星期五，她与 Karpathy 发布了一个播客。

sentences.en: It’s been watched nearly a million times.
sentences.zh: 它已被观看近百万次。

sentences.en: In the last six weeks, Guo and Gil have spoken with Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella, Meta founder Mark Zuckerberg, and Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan.
sentences.zh: 在过去的六周里，郭和吉尔与微软首席执行官萨蒂亚·纳德拉、Meta 创始人马克·扎克伯格和英特尔首席执行官陈立武进行了交谈。

blocks.html_en: The first guest she brought on No Priors was Noam Brown. Brown had built the AI system that beat the best human players at heads-up no-limit poker and one that learned to negotiate convincingly in the Diplomacy board game. Guo plays both and had been reading his papers for years. Brown is now at OpenAI and one of the people responsible for the test-time-inference techniques that took the frontier models from one-shot answers to extended reasoning, the shift that opened the way for agents. The evening I was at the office, he was at the poker event Conviction had laid on for their Embed founders.

sentences.en: The first guest she brought on No Priors was Noam Brown.
sentences.zh: 她在《No Priors》节目中带来的第一位嘉宾是诺姆·布朗 (Noam Brown)。

sentences.en: Brown had built the AI system that beat the best human players at heads-up no-limit poker and one that learned to negotiate convincingly in the Diplomacy board game.
sentences.zh: 布朗建立的人工智能系统能够在单挑无限注扑克中击败最优秀的人类玩家，并且学会在外交棋盘游戏中进行令人信服的谈判。

sentences.en: Guo plays both and had been reading his papers for years.
sentences.zh: 郭先生两者兼而有之，并且多年来一直在阅读他的论文。

sentences.en: Brown is now at OpenAI and one of the people responsible for the test-time-inference techniques that took the frontier models from one-shot answers to extended reasoning, the shift that opened the way for agents.
sentences.zh: Brown 现在是 OpenAI 的负责人之一，负责测试时推理技术，该技术将前沿模型从一次性答案转变为扩展推理，这一转变为智能体开辟了道路。

sentences.en: The evening I was at the office, he was at the poker event Conviction had laid on for their Embed founders.
sentences.zh: 我在办公室的那天晚上，他正在参加 Conviction 为 Embed 创始人举办的扑克活动。

blocks.html_en: “Sarah is a rare venture partner,” Jensen Huang told me. “She combines deep technical understanding with exceptional startup building insight. She speaks the language of entrepreneurs and is an absolute joy to work with.” Then he added, “Nvidia loves investing with her.”

sentences.en: “Sarah is a rare venture partner,” Jensen Huang told me.
sentences.zh: “莎拉是一位罕见的风险投资伙伴，”黄仁勋告诉我。

sentences.en: “She combines deep technical understanding with exceptional startup building insight.
sentences.zh: “她将深厚的技术理解与卓越的初创企业洞察力结合起来。

sentences.en: She speaks the language of entrepreneurs and is an absolute joy to work with.”
sentences.zh: 她会说企业家的语言，与她合作绝对是一种乐趣。”

sentences.en: Then he added, “Nvidia loves investing with her.”
sentences.zh: 然后他补充道，“英伟达喜欢和她一起投资。”

blocks.html_en: Conviction’s first check, before it even had a fund, went to two men in their pajamas: Gabe Pereyra, a worker ant out of Google Brain, DeepMind, and Meta, and Winston Weinberg, a USC-trained litigator at a white-shoe firm. They appeared over a crappy Zoom from a shared apartment in Los Angeles with no slides and no prototype, proposing to sell artificial intelligence to lawyers. “No one thought legal AI was interesting in any way, shape or form,” Weinberg recalls. “Zero.” Guo told them in the meeting that Conviction would be in. She was still raising the fund, so she wrote the seed check personally, and later folded the position into what would become Harvey.

sentences.en: Conviction’s first check, before it even had a fund, went to two men in their pajamas: Gabe Pereyra, a worker ant out of Google Brain, DeepMind, and Meta, and Winston Weinberg, a USC-trained litigator at a white-shoe firm.
sentences.zh: 在获得资金之前，Conviction 的第一笔支票发给了两名穿着睡衣的男子：​​加布·佩雷拉 (Gabe Pereyra)，一名来自 Google Brain、DeepMind 和 Meta 的工人蚂蚁；温斯顿·温伯格 (Winston Weinberg)，一名在南加州大学接受培训的诉讼律师，供职于一家白鞋公司。

sentences.en: They appeared over a crappy Zoom from a shared apartment in Los Angeles with no slides and no prototype, proposing to sell artificial intelligence to lawyers.
sentences.zh: 他们出现在洛杉矶一间合租公寓里的蹩脚 Zoom 上，没有幻灯片，也没有原型，提议向律师出售人工智能。

sentences.en: “No one thought legal AI was interesting in any way, shape or form,” Weinberg recalls.
sentences.zh: “没有人认为合法的人工智能在任何方面、形式或形式上都是有趣的，”温伯格回忆道。

sentences.en: “Zero.”
Guo told them in the meeting that Conviction would be in.
sentences.zh: 郭在会议上告诉他们定罪将会进入。

sentences.en: She was still raising the fund, so she wrote the seed check personally, and later folded the position into what would become Harvey.
sentences.zh: 她仍在筹集资金，因此她亲自写了种子支票，后来将该职位转入后来的 Harvey。

blocks.html_en: She owed the meeting to OpenAI. Ian Hathaway, who ran the lab’s startup fund, had sent the founders her way, and OpenAI invested beside her. That was 2022, when the labs still seeded the companies building on top of them—OpenAI also led the seed round in Cursor. They have since decided to build on top of themselves. Both Anthropic and OpenAI now sell legal tools of their own.

sentences.en: She owed the meeting to OpenAI.
sentences.zh: 这次会议归功于 OpenAI。

sentences.en: Ian Hathaway, who ran the lab’s startup fund, had sent the founders her way, and OpenAI invested beside her.
sentences.zh: 负责该实验室启动基金的伊恩·海瑟薇 (Ian Hathaway) 派出了几位创始人，OpenAI 也在她身边进行了投资。

sentences.en: That was 2022, when the labs still seeded the companies building on top of them—OpenAI also led the seed round in Cursor.
sentences.zh: 那是 2022 年，当时实验室仍在为在其基础上构建的公司提供种子轮——OpenAI 也在 Cursor 中领投了种子轮。

sentences.en: They have since decided to build on top of themselves.
sentences.zh: 从那以后，他们决定在自己的基础上再接再厉。

sentences.en: Both Anthropic and OpenAI now sell legal tools of their own.
sentences.zh: Anthropic 和 OpenAI 现在都在销售自己的法律工具。

blocks.html_en: Even so, four years on, Harvey is valued at $11 billion. Revenue has tripled in the past year, to $300 million. More than 100,000 lawyers run their work on it. Pereyra does not appear much troubled by the labs. Their tools are for individuals, he said, anyone with a contract to read. Harvey sells to law firms, who do not want to pour their secrets into a model used by the entire world. They want to own their models and their data. And they cannot depend on a single lab. A firm running only on Anthropic could never represent OpenAI, and if Anthropic fell behind, the firm would fall with it.

sentences.en: Even so, four years on, Harvey is valued at $11 billion.
sentences.zh: 即便如此，四年后，Harvey 的估值仍达到 110 亿美元。

sentences.en: Revenue has tripled in the past year, to $300 million.
sentences.zh: 去年收入增加了两倍，达到 3 亿美元。

sentences.en: More than 100,000 lawyers run their work on it.
sentences.zh: 超过 100,000 名律师在此开展工作。

sentences.en: Pereyra does not appear much troubled by the labs.
sentences.zh: 佩雷拉似乎并没有对实验室感到太多困扰。

sentences.en: Their tools are for individuals, he said, anyone with a contract to read.
sentences.zh: 他说，他们的工具适用于个人，任何有合同的人都可以阅读。

sentences.en: Harvey sells to law firms, who do not want to pour their secrets into a model used by the entire world.
sentences.zh: 哈维向律师事务所出售产品，而律师事务所不想将自己的秘密倾注到全世界使用的模型中。

sentences.en: They want to own their models and their data.
sentences.zh: 他们想拥有自己的模型和数据。

sentences.en: And they cannot depend on a single lab.
sentences.zh: 他们不能依赖于单一实验室。

sentences.en: A firm running only on Anthropic could never represent OpenAI, and if Anthropic fell behind, the firm would fall with it.
sentences.zh: 一家仅在 Anthropic 上运行的公司永远无法代表 OpenAI，如果 Anthropic 落后了，该公司也会随之倒下。

blocks.html_en: This, he said, was how you won in venture. You sucked the oxygen out of the air.

sentences.en: This, he said, was how you won in venture.
sentences.zh: 他说，这就是你在风险投资中获胜的方式。

sentences.en: You sucked the oxygen out of the air.
sentences.zh: 你从空气中吸走了氧气。

blocks.html_en: This, he said, was how you won in venture. You sucked the oxygen out of the air.

sentences.en: This, he said, was how you won in venture.
sentences.zh: 他说，这就是你在风险投资中获胜的方式。

sentences.en: You sucked the oxygen out of the air.
sentences.zh: 你从空气中吸走了氧气。

blocks.html_en: What Harvey sells to law firms, underneath the software, is trust, and trust is delivered by humans. So the AI company built to do the work of lawyers has hired 200 lawyers of its own, many of whom teach other lawyers, in person, how to let the software do their job. Guo told me this is what much of Conviction’s portfolio is finding: as the models keep getting better, the work of helping a human get value out of them keeps getting bigger.

sentences.en: What Harvey sells to law firms, underneath the software, is trust, and trust is delivered by humans.
sentences.zh: 哈维在软件的底层向律师事务所出售的是信任，而信任是由人类传递的。

sentences.en: So the AI company built to do the work of lawyers has hired 200 lawyers of its own, many of whom teach other lawyers, in person, how to let the software do their job.
sentences.zh: 因此，这家专为律师工作而建立的人工智能公司雇佣了 200 名自己的律师，其中许多人亲自教导其他律师如何让软件完成他们的工作。

sentences.en: Guo told me this is what much of Conviction’s portfolio is finding: as the models keep getting better, the work of helping a human get value out of them keeps getting bigger.
sentences.zh: 郭告诉我，这就是 Conviction 的大部分投资组合所发现的：随着模型不断变得更好，帮助人类从中获取价值的工作也不断扩大。

blocks.html_en: Behind Harvey, at every turn, is Guo. It was she who won the firm its first client, the global law firm A&O Shearman. She had spoken about the company at Stanford’s business school, and a lawyer in the audience was moved to join Harvey and sell it to his old firm. When Harvey needed a chief business officer, she found John Haddock, a trained lawyer with a decade at Stripe. And when the Valley’s best companies began ripping their products apart to rebuild them around agents, or started training their own models with the inference companies, Guo had seen it coming “way, way earlier” than the other investors and walked Harvey through the changes. “She’s just so in the weeds,” Weinberg added. “She really knows what’s happening at all these companies from the ground level.”

sentences.en: Behind Harvey, at every turn, is Guo.
sentences.zh: 哈维的身后，每一个转折点都是郭。

sentences.en: It was she who won the firm its first client, the global law firm A&O Shearman.
sentences.zh: 正是她为该公司赢得了第一个客户，即全球律师事务所 A&O Shearman。

sentences.en: She had spoken about the company at Stanford’s business school, and a lawyer in the audience was moved to join Harvey and sell it to his old firm.
sentences.zh: 她在斯坦福大学商学院谈到了这家公司，听众中的一位律师被感动加入哈维并将其出售给他的老公司。

sentences.en: When Harvey needed a chief business officer, she found John Haddock, a trained lawyer with a decade at Stripe.
sentences.zh: 当哈维需要一位首席商务官时，她找到了约翰·哈多克（John Haddock），他是一位训练有素的律师，在 Stripe 工作了十年。

sentences.en: And when the Valley’s best companies began ripping their products apart to rebuild them around agents, or started training their own models with the inference companies, Guo had seen it coming “way, way earlier” than the other investors and walked Harvey through the changes.
sentences.zh: 当硅谷最好的公司开始拆分他们的产品，围绕代理进行重建，或者开始与推理公司一起训练自己的模型时，郭比其他投资者“早得多”地看到了这一点，并带领哈维经历了这些变化。

sentences.en: “She’s just so in the weeds,” Weinberg added.
sentences.zh: “她简直就是在杂草丛中，”温伯格补充道。

sentences.en: “She really knows what’s happening at all these companies from the ground level.”
sentences.zh: “她真的从底层了解所有这些公司正在发生的事情。”

blocks.html_en: Nowhere is that more true than at Baseten, an inference company Harvey works closely with, and the investment Guo is best known for. She first backed it in 2019, three years before ChatGPT. Inference is what AI does once it has been trained. If a model is a lawyer, training is law school and inference is the job itself: reviewing contracts, depositions, generally doing what a lawyer was taught. Every Claude query is inference. Every Harvey contract markup is inference. Every Tesla rerouted to a Whole Foods for magnesium supplements is inference.

sentences.en: Nowhere is that more true than at Baseten, an inference company Harvey works closely with, and the investment Guo is best known for.
sentences.zh: 没有比 Baseten 更能体现这一点的了，这是一家与 Harvey 密切合作的推理公司，郭先生也以投资而闻名。

sentences.en: She first backed it in 2019, three years before ChatGPT.
sentences.zh: 她于 2019 年首次支持该项目，比 ChatGPT 早三年。

sentences.en: Inference is what AI does once it has been trained.
sentences.zh: 推理是人工智能经过训练后所做的事情。

sentences.en: If a model is a lawyer, training is law school and inference is the job itself: reviewing contracts, depositions, generally doing what a lawyer was taught.
sentences.zh: 如果模特是律师，培训就是法学院，推理就是工作本身：审查合同、证词，通常做律师所教的事情。

sentences.en: Every Claude query is inference.
sentences.zh: 克劳德的每一个查询都是推理。

sentences.en: Every Harvey contract markup is inference.
sentences.zh: 每个哈维合同加价都是推断。

sentences.en: Every Tesla rerouted to a Whole Foods for magnesium supplements is inference.
sentences.zh: 每一辆特斯拉转向全食超市购买镁补充剂都是推论。

blocks.html_en: No AI company runs without it, and the labs sell it by the token the way power companies sell electricity by the kilowatt-hour. Which means that any company building on Anthropic or OpenAI is, in the end, beholden to them for prices, performance, and the next month’s roadmap. This is the water everyone is tired of drinking. An ecosystem beyond the labs needs its own supply and Baseten is a supplier. It lets companies run their own models, including the open-source ones now coming out of China and Europe, some of which perform within striking distance of the frontier and cost a fraction as much. As the big labs have raised token prices this year, in some cases by 100x, demand for Baseten has gone vertical.

sentences.en: No AI company runs without it, and the labs sell it by the token the way power companies sell electricity by the kilowatt-hour.
sentences.zh: 没有人工智能公司可以在没有它的情况下运行，实验室以代币形式出售它，就像电力公司按千瓦时出售电力一样。

sentences.en: Which means that any company building on Anthropic or OpenAI is, in the end, beholden to them for prices, performance, and the next month’s roadmap.
sentences.zh: 这意味着任何基于 Anthropic 或 OpenAI 的公司最终都会在价格、性能和下个月的路线图上受到它们的影响。

sentences.en: This is the water everyone is tired of drinking.
sentences.zh: 这是每个人都喝腻了的水。

sentences.en: An ecosystem beyond the labs needs its own supply and Baseten is a supplier.
sentences.zh: 实验室之外的生态系统需要自己的供应，而 Baseten 就是供应商。

sentences.en: It lets companies run their own models, including the open-source ones now coming out of China and Europe, some of which perform within striking distance of the frontier and cost a fraction as much.
sentences.zh: 它让公司可以运行自己的模型，包括现在来自中国和欧洲的开源模型，其中一些模型的性能接近前沿，而且成本仅为前沿的一小部分。

sentences.en: As the big labs have raised token prices this year, in some cases by 100x, demand for Baseten has gone vertical.
sentences.zh: 随着今年大型实验室提高了代币价格，在某些情况下提高了 100 倍，对 Baseten 的需求已经垂直增长。

blocks.html_en: Revenue has grown 20-fold in the past 12 months. Inference volume has grown 40-fold. In January, Baseten raised $300 million at a $5 billion valuation. Five months later, it raised another $1.5 billion, this time at a $13 billion price tag. It was the fourth raise in 18 months. Four years ago, Tuhin Srivastava, its founder, was building for a market that did not yet exist.

sentences.en: Revenue has grown 20-fold in the past 12 months.
sentences.zh: 过去 12 个月收入增长了 20 倍。

sentences.en: Inference volume has grown 40-fold.
sentences.zh: 推理量增长了 40 倍。

sentences.en: In January, Baseten raised $300 million at a $5 billion valuation.
sentences.zh: 一月份，Baseten 筹集了 3 亿美元，估值为 50 亿美元。

sentences.en: Five months later, it raised another $1.5 billion, this time at a $13 billion price tag.
sentences.zh: 五个月后，它又筹集了 15 亿美元，这次的标价为 130 亿美元。

sentences.en: It was the fourth raise in 18 months.
sentences.zh: 这是 18 个月内第四次加薪。

sentences.en: Four years ago, Tuhin Srivastava, its founder, was building for a market that did not yet exist.
sentences.zh: 四年前，其创始人 Tuhin Srivastava 正在为一个尚不存在的市场进行建设。

blocks.html_en: “You guys seem great,” Guo told Srivastava the first time she met him, in 2014. “This idea is bad. But if you ever do something else, please call me.” She was then a principal at Greylock. The idea was a machine-learning startup for healthcare. He pivoted, sold it, called her again five years later. The new business was Baseten. The category, then called MLOps, was, as Guo put it, “not a great category from that vintage.” She co-led the seed round anyway. “I really loved the team,” she said. “I felt that they were just fundamentally correct.”

sentences.en: “You guys seem great,” Guo told Srivastava the first time she met him, in 2014.
sentences.zh: “你们看起来很棒，”郭在 2014 年第一次见到 Srivastava 时告诉他。

sentences.en: “This idea is bad.
sentences.zh: “这个想法很糟糕。

sentences.en: But if you ever do something else, please call me.”
sentences.zh: 但如果你有别的事，请给我打电话。”

sentences.en: She was then a principal at Greylock.
sentences.zh: 当时她是格雷洛克学校的校长。

sentences.en: The idea was a machine-learning startup for healthcare.
sentences.zh: 这个想法是一家医疗保健机器学习初创公司。

sentences.en: He pivoted, sold it, called her again five years later.
sentences.zh: 他转身，卖掉了它，五年后再次给她打电话。

sentences.en: The new business was Baseten.
sentences.zh: 新业务是 Baseten。

sentences.en: The category, then called MLOps, was, as Guo put it, “not a great category from that vintage.”
sentences.zh: 正如郭所说，这个当时被称为 MLOps 的类别“在那个年代并不是一个伟大的类别”。

sentences.en: She co-led the seed round anyway.
sentences.zh: 无论如何，她共同领导了种子轮。

sentences.en: “I really loved the team,” she said.
sentences.zh: “我真的很喜欢这个团队，”她说。

sentences.en: “I felt that they were just fundamentally correct.”
sentences.zh: “我觉得他们从根本上来说是正确的。”

blocks.html_en: For three years Baseten made almost no money. “I bet you she was worried,” Srivastava told me. “But she didn’t show it. She never wavered.” Late in 2022, in the months between Guo’s leaving Greylock and the founding of Conviction, Baseten was trying to hire an engineer in New York. Guo flew across the country and took the engineer to Carbone, on Thompson Street, for a four-hour lunch. Two years before that, six months into the pandemic, Srivastava and his wife had been going slowly mad in a one-bedroom apartment in San Francisco. Guo offered them her house. She and Grady were going away for a few days; he should stay. “It’s not like it was some empty holiday home they don’t use,” Srivastava told me. “It was their home and she gave it to us for a week.”

sentences.en: For three years Baseten made almost no money.
sentences.zh: 三年来，巴斯​​坦几乎没有赚到钱。

sentences.en: “I bet you she was worried,” Srivastava told me.
sentences.zh: “我敢打赌她很担心，”斯里瓦斯塔瓦告诉我。

sentences.en: “But she didn’t show it.
sentences.zh: “但她没有表现出来。

sentences.en: She never wavered.”
sentences.zh: 她从未动摇过。”

sentences.en: Late in 2022, in the months between Guo’s leaving Greylock and the founding of Conviction, Baseten was trying to hire an engineer in New York.
sentences.zh: 2022 年末，在郭离开 Greylock 和 Conviction 成立之间的几个月里，Baseten 试图在纽约聘请一名工程师。

sentences.en: Guo flew across the country and took the engineer to Carbone, on Thompson Street, for a four-hour lunch.
sentences.zh: 郭飞越全国，带工程师到汤普森街的卡本吃了四个小时的午餐。

sentences.en: Two years before that, six months into the pandemic, Srivastava and his wife had been going slowly mad in a one-bedroom apartment in San Francisco.
sentences.zh: 两年前，也就是疫情爆发六个月后，斯里瓦斯塔瓦和他的妻子在旧金山的一间卧室公寓里慢慢地陷入了疯狂。

sentences.en: Guo offered them her house.
sentences.zh: 郭向他们提供了她的房子。

sentences.en: She and Grady were going away for a few days; he should stay.
sentences.zh: 她和格雷迪要离开几天；他应该留下来。

sentences.en: “It’s not like it was some empty holiday home they don’t use,” Srivastava told me.
sentences.zh: “这不像是他们不使用的空置度假屋，”斯里瓦斯塔瓦告诉我。

sentences.en: “It was their home and she gave it to us for a week.”
sentences.zh: “这是他们的家，她把它给了我们一周。”

blocks.html_en: “Over seven years, she’s shown up on a daily basis like this,” Srivastava said. “If I texted her right now and said, ‘Can you talk to a candidate today?’ she’d be like, ‘What’s their number?’ And she’ll do it within an hour.”

sentences.en: “Over seven years, she’s shown up on a daily basis like this,” Srivastava said.
sentences.zh: “七年来，她每天都像这样出现，”斯里瓦斯塔瓦说。

sentences.en: “If I texted her right now and said, ‘Can you talk to a candidate today?’
sentences.zh: “如果我现在给她发短信说，‘你今天能和候选人谈谈吗？’

sentences.en: she’d be like, ‘What’s their number?’
sentences.zh: 她会问，“他们的电话号码是多少？”

sentences.en: And she’ll do it within an hour.”
sentences.zh: 她会在一小时内完成。”

blocks.html_en: She led the Series A in 2022 and co-led the $1.5 billion Series F this year. In between, she invested in every round, each check bigger than the last. Through it all, she and Srivastava have sparred constantly. “We argue all the time about what the priority is for the business,” Srivastava said. “It’s always very, very constructive.” The other 60% of his conversations with Guo, he said, are bantering. “Her ability to be a person but also a very competent professional in an incredibly tumultuous industry,” he vouched, “is wild.”

sentences.en: She led the Series A in 2022 and co-led the $1.5 billion Series F this year.
sentences.zh: 她在 2022 年领投了 A 轮融资，并在今年共同领投了 15 亿美元的 F 轮融资。

sentences.en: In between, she invested in every round, each check bigger than the last.
sentences.zh: 在此期间，她投资了每一轮，每张支票都比上一张更大。

sentences.en: Through it all, she and Srivastava have sparred constantly.
sentences.zh: 自始至终，她和斯里瓦斯塔瓦一直在争吵。

sentences.en: “We argue all the time about what the priority is for the business,” Srivastava said.
sentences.zh: “我们一直在争论业务的优先事项是什么，”斯里瓦斯塔瓦说。

sentences.en: “It’s always very, very constructive.”
sentences.zh: “这总是非常非常有建设性的。”

sentences.en: The other 60% of his conversations with Guo, he said, are bantering.
sentences.zh: 他说，他与郭的另外 60% 的谈话都是开玩笑。

sentences.en: “Her ability to be a person but also a very competent professional in an incredibly tumultuous industry,” he vouched, “is wild.”
sentences.zh: “在这个极其动荡的行业中，她既能成为一个人，又能成为一名非常有能力的专业人士，”他保证道，“这是疯狂的。”

blocks.html_en: Srivastava had last spoken to Guo eight hours before we talked; he expected to speak to her again in three. The night before, at 8:30pm, she had been on the phone with Baseten’s chief financial officer. At 10pm, Srivastava texted her to ask how she was.

sentences.en: Srivastava had last spoken to Guo eight hours before we talked; he expected to speak to her again in three.
sentences.zh: 斯里瓦斯塔瓦最后一次与郭通话是在我们通话前八小时。他预计三点后再次和她说话。

sentences.en: The night before, at 8:30pm, she had been on the phone with Baseten’s chief financial officer.
sentences.zh: 前一天晚上 8:30，她一直在与 Baseten 的首席财务官通电话。

sentences.en: At 10pm, Srivastava texted her to ask how she was.
sentences.zh: 晚上 10 点，斯里瓦斯塔瓦给她发短信询问她怎么样。

blocks.html_en: “My VO2 max isn’t high enough,” she replied.

sentences.en: “My VO2 max isn’t high enough,” she replied.
sentences.zh: “我的最大摄氧量不够高，”她回答道。

blocks.html_en: It was Guo who introduced Srivastava to Huang. In January, Nvidia put $150 million into Baseten’s $300 million Series E. Srivastava credits Guo with more than $1 billion of the company’s value.

sentences.en: It was Guo who introduced Srivastava to Huang.
sentences.zh: 是郭将斯里瓦斯塔瓦介绍给了黄。

sentences.en: In January, Nvidia put $150 million into Baseten’s $300 million Series E.
sentences.zh: 一月份，Nvidia 向 Baseten 的 3 亿美元 E 轮融资投资了 1.5 亿美元。

sentences.en: Srivastava credits Guo with more than $1 billion of the company’s value.
sentences.zh: Srivastava 认为郭先生对公司价值超过 10 亿美元的贡献。

blocks.html_en: “The company would feel fundamentally different without Sarah,” Dannie Herzberg, Baseten’s president, told me. “She is a core part of the team. It’s actually quite remarkable. I imagine every one of her portfolio companies would say the same.”

sentences.en: “The company would feel fundamentally different without Sarah,” Dannie Herzberg, Baseten’s president, told me.
sentences.zh: Baseten 总裁丹尼·赫茨伯格 (Dannie Herzberg) 告诉我：“如果没有莎拉，公司的感觉会发生根本性的变化。”

sentences.en: “She is a core part of the team.
sentences.zh: “她是团队的核心成员。

sentences.en: It’s actually quite remarkable.
sentences.zh: 这实际上是非常了不起的。

sentences.en: I imagine every one of her portfolio companies would say the same.”
sentences.zh: 我想她投资的每一家公司都会这么说。”

blocks.html_en: Bret Taylor, who co-founded Sierra, one of Conviction’s earliest investments, is not a man short of people to call. He does not call many investors, but he calls Guo because “she has her ear to the ground on both talent and market momentum,” he said. He will run her through the state of his business and ask what he’s missing, and she’ll tell him a competitor does some specific thing better, and the next day he’ll have three notes from her because she spent the evening on follow-up calls with other companies learning more. By the end of it all, his product roadmap has changed.

sentences.en: Bret Taylor, who co-founded Sierra, one of Conviction’s earliest investments, is not a man short of people to call.
sentences.zh: 布雷特·泰勒 (Bret Taylor) 是 Conviction 最早的投资项目之一 Sierra 的联合创始人，他并不缺少可以打电话的人。

sentences.en: He does not call many investors, but he calls Guo because “she has her ear to the ground on both talent and market momentum,” he said.
sentences.zh: 他没有给很多投资者打电话，但他给郭打电话，因为“她对人才和市场势头都很了解，”他说。

sentences.en: He will run her through the state of his business and ask what he’s missing, and she’ll tell him a competitor does some specific thing better, and the next day he’ll have three notes from her because she spent the evening on follow-up calls with other companies learning more.
sentences.zh: 他会向她介绍他的业务状况并询问他缺少什么，她会告诉他竞争对手在某些具体事情上做得更好，第二天他会收到她的三张笔记，因为她花了一个晚上与其他公司进行后续电话了解更多信息。

sentences.en: By the end of it all, his product roadmap has changed.
sentences.zh: 最终，他的产品路线图发生了变化。

blocks.html_en: Taylor also chairs the board of OpenAI. The fight, up close, is not so clean. Guo talks to everyone, all the time—that is the job—and the lines cross wherever you look. Vernal’s wife runs product at Anthropic; her husband competes with her for deals; and the man calling her for market intelligence governs one of the biggest labs.

sentences.en: Taylor also chairs the board of OpenAI.
sentences.zh: 泰勒还担任 OpenAI 董事会主席。

sentences.en: The fight, up close, is not so clean.
sentences.zh: 近距离的战斗并不那么干净。

sentences.en: Guo talks to everyone, all the time—that is the job—and the lines cross wherever you look.
sentences.zh: 郭一直在与每个人交谈——这就是工作——无论你往哪里看，界限都是交叉的。

sentences.en: Vernal’s wife runs product at Anthropic; her husband competes with her for deals; and the man calling her for market intelligence governs one of the biggest labs.
sentences.zh: Vernal 的妻子负责 Anthropic 的产品运营；她的丈夫与她争夺交易；打电话给她询问市场情报的那个人管理着最大的实验室之一。

blocks.html_en: You could characterize some of what I’ve been trying to do for a long time as I just want to get to the center of it, whatever the next thing is.

sentences.en: You could characterize some of what I’ve been trying to do for a long time as I just want to get to the center of it, whatever the next thing is.
sentences.zh: 你可以描述我长期以来一直在尝试做的一些事情，因为我只想到达它的中心，无论接下来的事情是什么。

blocks.html_en: You could characterize some of what I’ve been trying to do for a long time as I just want to get to the center of it, whatever the next thing is.

sentences.en: You could characterize some of what I’ve been trying to do for a long time as I just want to get to the center of it, whatever the next thing is.
sentences.zh: 你可以描述我长期以来一直在尝试做的一些事情，因为我只想到达它的中心，无论接下来的事情是什么。

blocks.html_en: For a fund Conviction’s size they have not lost many deals they wanted. It’s because, Reddy said, they outwork everyone. “There’s just always more to be done.” The word people use about Guo is “intense.” They clarify they mean it as a compliment, and then they tell some version of the same story. The canonical version is the one Grady shared on Twitter a few years ago.

sentences.en: For a fund Conviction’s size they have not lost many deals they wanted.
sentences.zh: 对于 Conviction 的规模来说，他们并没有失去很多他们想要的交易。

sentences.en: It’s because, Reddy said, they outwork everyone.
sentences.zh: 雷迪说，这是因为他们比所有人都更努力。

sentences.en: “There’s just always more to be done.”
sentences.zh: “总是有更多事情要做。”

sentences.en: The word people use about Guo is “intense.”
sentences.zh: 人们对郭的评价是“激烈”。

sentences.en: They clarify they mean it as a compliment, and then they tell some version of the same story.
sentences.zh: 他们澄清说这是一种恭维，然后他们讲述了同一个故事的某个版本。

sentences.en: The canonical version is the one Grady shared on Twitter a few years ago.
sentences.zh: 规范版本是 Grady 几年前在 Twitter 上分享的版本。

blocks.html_en: He and Guo had driven up to wine country one Saturday. They lived in the South Bay at the time. The drive was two hours. There were three or four appointments at the other end. Five minutes in, with Grady at the wheel, Guo asked if she could make a call. Sure, he said. They had two hours. “So she gets on the phone,” Grady recalled, “and for the whole two-hour drive, there was one phone call, then another phone call, then another phone call, and then another phone call.”

sentences.en: He and Guo had driven up to wine country one Saturday.
sentences.zh: 一个星期六，他和郭开车去了葡萄酒之乡。

sentences.en: They lived in the South Bay at the time.
sentences.zh: 当时他们住在南湾。

sentences.en: The drive was two hours.
sentences.zh: 车程两个小时。

sentences.en: There were three or four appointments at the other end.
sentences.zh: 另一端有三四个约会。

sentences.en: Five minutes in, with Grady at the wheel, Guo asked if she could make a call.
sentences.zh: 五分钟后，格雷迪开车，郭问她是否可以打个电话。

sentences.en: Sure, he said.
sentences.zh: 当然，他说。

sentences.en: They had two hours.
sentences.zh: 他们有两个小时。

sentences.en: “So she gets on the phone,” Grady recalled, “and for the whole two-hour drive, there was one phone call, then another phone call, then another phone call, and then another phone call.”
sentences.zh: “于是她就打电话了，”格雷迪回忆道，“在整个两个小时的车程中，接到一个电话，然后又一个电话，然后又一个电话，然后又一个电话。”

blocks.html_en: She was on the phone between appointments. That night she stayed up writing a memo. The next morning she was on a call with her partners. A week later, Grady learned she had won the deal against Sequoia, against Andreessen Horowitz, against Benchmark, against everyone in the round. This, he said, was how you won in venture. You sucked the oxygen out of the air.

sentences.en: She was on the phone between appointments.
sentences.zh: 她在两次约会之间打电话。

sentences.en: That night she stayed up writing a memo.
sentences.zh: 那天晚上，她熬夜写了一份备忘录。

sentences.en: The next morning she was on a call with her partners.
sentences.zh: 第二天早上，她正在和她的合作伙伴通电话。

sentences.en: A week later, Grady learned she had won the deal against Sequoia, against Andreessen Horowitz, against Benchmark, against everyone in the round.
sentences.zh: 一周后，格雷迪得知她已经战胜了红杉资本、安德森霍洛维茨、Benchmark、以及本轮中的所有人，赢得了这笔交易。

sentences.en: This, he said, was how you won in venture.
sentences.zh: 他说，这就是你在风险投资中获胜的方式。

sentences.en: You sucked the oxygen out of the air.
sentences.zh: 你从空气中吸走了氧气。

blocks.html_en: It took Grady a week to learn what he had been a witness to. The couple have spent their married life on opposite sides of the same business and, as such, they keep a strict wall between them. They do not discuss the deals they are working on. In the car that weekend she had been vague about the company. Weinberg, Harvey’s co-founder, has both Grady and Guo as investors. He told me he had not believed them about their wall. “I am one of the least trusting people ever,” he said. “They have proven me otherwise.”

sentences.en: It took Grady a week to learn what he had been a witness to.
sentences.zh: 格雷迪花了一周的时间才了解他所目睹的一切。

sentences.en: The couple have spent their married life on opposite sides of the same business and, as such, they keep a strict wall between them.
sentences.zh: 这对夫妇在同一家企业的不同方面度过了婚姻生活，因此，他们之间保持着严格的隔离墙。

sentences.en: They do not discuss the deals they are working on.
sentences.zh: 他们不讨论他们正在进行的交易。

sentences.en: In the car that weekend she had been vague about the company.
sentences.zh: 那个周末在车里她对公司的情况含糊其辞。

sentences.en: Weinberg, Harvey’s co-founder, has both Grady and Guo as investors.
sentences.zh: 哈维联合创始人温伯格的投资者是格雷迪和郭。

sentences.en: He told me he had not believed them about their wall.
sentences.zh: 他告诉我他不相信他们关于他们的墙的说法。

sentences.en: “I am one of the least trusting people ever,” he said.
sentences.zh: “我是有史以来最不信任别人的人之一，”他说。

sentences.en: “They have proven me otherwise.”
sentences.zh: “他们已经向我证明了，事实并非如此。”

blocks.html_en: The company that weekend was Remotion. The founders were Alex Embiricos and Charley Ho, out of Dropbox and Google respectively. Guo had been introduced one shade late in the process. The founders were, in her words, “central casting.” She had wanted to make sure they only talked to her, and so she had spent the drive doing two things. The first was finding people who could vouch for her. The second was simpler. “If you’re just talking to me the entire weekend,” she said, “you can’t talk to anybody else. So that’s helpful. It’s like filibustering.”

sentences.en: The company that weekend was Remotion.
sentences.zh: 那个周末的公司是Remotion。

sentences.en: The founders were Alex Embiricos and Charley Ho, out of Dropbox and Google respectively.
sentences.zh: 创始人是 Alex Embiricos 和 Charley Ho，分别来自 Dropbox 和 Google。

sentences.en: Guo had been introduced one shade late in the process.
sentences.zh: 郭是在这个过程的后期才被引入的。

sentences.en: The founders were, in her words, “central casting.”
sentences.zh: 用她的话说，创始人是“核心人物”。

sentences.en: She had wanted to make sure they only talked to her, and so she had spent the drive doing two things.
sentences.zh: 她想确保他们只和她说话，所以她在开车的路上做了两件事。

sentences.en: The first was finding people who could vouch for her.
sentences.zh: 首先是找到可以为她担保的人。

sentences.en: The second was simpler.
sentences.zh: 第二个更简单。

sentences.en: “If you’re just talking to me the entire weekend,” she said, “you can’t talk to anybody else.
sentences.zh: “如果你整个周末都在和我说话，”她说，“你就无法和其他人说话。

sentences.en: So that’s helpful.
sentences.zh: 这很有帮助。

sentences.en: It’s like filibustering.”
sentences.zh: 这就像阻挠议事。”

blocks.html_en: It had been her and Grady’s wedding anniversary. “Pat is very tolerant of me,” she said. “I don’t think he was thrilled with being an Uber driver. It might have been tough.”

sentences.en: It had been her and Grady’s wedding anniversary.
sentences.zh: 那是她和格雷迪的结婚纪念日。

sentences.en: “Pat is very tolerant of me,” she said.
sentences.zh: “帕特对我非常宽容，”她说。

sentences.en: “I don’t think he was thrilled with being an Uber driver.
sentences.zh: “我认为他对成为一名 Uber 司机并不感到兴奋。

sentences.en: It might have been tough.”
sentences.zh: 可能会很艰难。”

blocks.html_en: OpenAI has since bought Remotion.

sentences.en: OpenAI has since bought Remotion.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 此后收购了 Remotion。

blocks.html_en: “She is indefatigable, just always working,” Vernal said. “I get up at 4:45am, but I go to bed at like 9:30pm. Sarah’s up at 4:45am and goes to bed at midnight.” Guo hasn’t watched TV in 15 years, she told me, and doesn’t hang out socially the way other investors do. When she’s not working, she likes to combine two activities into one. She recently completed an 85-mile bike race with over 4,000 meters of climbing through the Dolomites in Italy. “It’s a dual-purpose thing,” she had said, “because I’ll be spending time with my husband and that’s my workout for the year.”

sentences.en: “She is indefatigable, just always working,” Vernal said.
sentences.zh: “她不知疲倦，总是在工作，”弗纳尔说。

sentences.en: “I get up at 4:45am, but I go to bed at like 9:30pm.
sentences.zh: “我早上 4:45 起床，但晚上 9:30 左右睡觉。

sentences.en: Sarah’s up at 4:45am and goes to bed at midnight.”
sentences.zh: 莎拉凌晨 4 点 45 分起床，半夜上床睡觉。”

sentences.en: Guo hasn’t watched TV in 15 years, she told me, and doesn’t hang out socially the way other investors do.
sentences.zh: 她告诉我，郭已经 15 年没有看电视了，也不像其他投资者那样参加社交活动。

sentences.en: When she’s not working, she likes to combine two activities into one.
sentences.zh: 当她不工作时，她喜欢将两项活动合二为一。

sentences.en: She recently completed an 85-mile bike race with over 4,000 meters of climbing through the Dolomites in Italy.
sentences.zh: 她最近完成了一场 85 英里的自行车比赛，其中攀爬了 4,000 多米，穿越了意大利的多洛米蒂山脉。

sentences.en: “It’s a dual-purpose thing,” she had said, “because I’ll be spending time with my husband and that’s my workout for the year.”
sentences.zh: “这是一个双重目的的事情，”她说，“因为我会花时间和我的丈夫在一起，这就是我今年的锻炼。”

blocks.html_en: In the eight weeks between my two interviews with Guo, she had her fourth child with Grady, walked the Met Gala carpet in 45 pounds of custom chainmail with her mother, recorded multiple podcasts, and was still answering her founders’ texts within minutes.

sentences.en: In the eight weeks between my two interviews with Guo, she had her fourth child with Grady, walked the Met Gala carpet in 45 pounds of custom chainmail with her mother, recorded multiple podcasts, and was still answering her founders’ texts within minutes.
sentences.zh: 在我对郭的两次采访之间的八周内，她和格雷迪生下了第四个孩子，和母亲一起穿着 45 磅的定制链甲走在 Met Gala 地毯上，录制了多个播客，并且仍在几分钟内回复创始人的短信。

blocks.html_en: “I’m lucky to have a wonderful partner in Pat who is also a ‘more is more’ person,” Guo said. “We had a fourth kid and the week after he said, ‘So do you want another daughter or another dog?’ But this time I’m like, ‘This is not a company. We don’t just keep growing. We’re done growing here.’”

sentences.en: “I’m lucky to have a wonderful partner in Pat who is also a ‘more is more’ person,” Guo said.
sentences.zh: “我很幸运能有一个出色的合作伙伴帕特，他也是一个‘多即是多’的人，”郭说。

sentences.en: “We had a fourth kid and the week after he said, ‘So do you want another daughter or another dog?’
sentences.zh: “我们有了第四个孩子，一周后他说，‘那么你想要另一个女儿还是另一只狗吗？’

sentences.en: But this time I’m like, ‘This is not a company.
sentences.zh: 但这次我想，‘这不是一家公司。

sentences.en: We don’t just keep growing.
sentences.zh: 我们不只是不断成长。

sentences.en: We’re done growing here.’”
sentences.zh: 我们已经不再在这里成长了。’”

blocks.html_en: The first video game Guo ever played was her father’s, and it involved killing Bill Gates. She was six or seven, sitting at a Linux machine in the basement of a house where her father had recently and audibly delighted in the fact that he could run his own server. The game was called XBill (like Kill Bill) and the player’s job was to stop little bespectacled Bill clones from installing Windows on the screen.

sentences.en: The first video game Guo ever played was her father’s, and it involved killing Bill Gates.
sentences.zh: 郭玩的第一款电子游戏是她父亲的，其中涉及杀死比尔·盖茨。

sentences.en: She was six or seven, sitting at a Linux machine in the basement of a house where her father had recently and audibly delighted in the fact that he could run his own server.
sentences.zh: 她当时六七岁，坐在一所房子地下室的一台 Linux 机器前，她的父亲最近很高兴能够运行自己的服务器。

sentences.en: The game was called XBill (like Kill Bill) and the player’s job was to stop little bespectacled Bill clones from installing Windows on the screen.
sentences.zh: 这款游戏名为 XBill（类似于《杀死比尔》），玩家的任务是阻止戴眼镜的比尔克隆人在屏幕上安装 Windows。

blocks.html_en: “There is a thread of technical rebellion in my dad’s ethos that I have carried,” she told me. “It’s like, we’re going to win against the man!”

sentences.en: “There is a thread of technical rebellion in my dad’s ethos that I have carried,” she told me.
sentences.zh: “我继承了我父亲的精神气质中的技术叛逆精神，”她告诉我。

sentences.en: “It’s like, we’re going to win against the man!”
sentences.zh: “就像，我们要赢那个男人了！”

blocks.html_en: By the time she was a teenager her father had a company of his own, a maker of broadband equipment called Casa Systems, out of Andover, Massachusetts, that would in time power large fractions of the internet in cities like New York. Guo grew up inside it. At 14 she built Casa’s first website under the supervision of Doug Rosich, the company’s head of hardware engineering, whom she remembers as terrifying. The website made heavy use of gradients, which were just then coming into fashion on the internet. She did her homework in an office cubicle. She slept over for bug bashes. With her godfather, Casa’s chief technology officer, she built castles out of empty Diet Coke cans. By 19 she was pitching the place to investors.

sentences.en: By the time she was a teenager her father had a company of his own, a maker of broadband equipment called Casa Systems, out of Andover, Massachusetts, that would in time power large fractions of the internet in cities like New York.
sentences.zh: 当她十几岁的时候，她的父亲就拥有了一家自己的公司，一家名为 Casa Systems 的宽带设备制造商，位于马​​萨诸塞州安多佛，最终将为纽约等城市的大部分互联网提供电力。

sentences.en: Guo grew up inside it.
sentences.zh: 郭先生就是在里面长大的。

sentences.en: At 14 she built Casa’s first website under the supervision of Doug Rosich, the company’s head of hardware engineering, whom she remembers as terrifying.
sentences.zh: 14 岁时，她在公司硬件工程主管道格·罗西奇 (Doug Rosich) 的监督下建立了 Casa 的第一个网站，她记得罗西奇是个可怕的人。

sentences.en: The website made heavy use of gradients, which were just then coming into fashion on the internet.
sentences.zh: 该网站大量使用了当时在互联网上流行的渐变色。

sentences.en: She did her homework in an office cubicle.
sentences.zh: 她在办公室的小隔间里做作业。

sentences.en: She slept over for bug bashes.
sentences.zh: 她为了消灭虫子而睡过觉。

sentences.en: With her godfather, Casa’s chief technology officer, she built castles out of empty Diet Coke cans.
sentences.zh: 她和她的教父、Casa 的首席技术官一起，用空的健怡可乐罐建造了城堡。

sentences.en: By 19 she was pitching the place to investors.
sentences.zh: 19 岁时，她就向投资者推销这个地方。

blocks.html_en: Her father, Jerry Guo, had come to America from Hunan in 1987. He was 24 with $50 and a transcript from Tsinghua, having scored first on the gaokao, the exam by which Chinese society sorts its 18-year-olds. Her mother, Lucy Xie, an engineer, followed a year later. Bell Labs hired them both. Jerry could have stayed for life. Instead, as the dot-com boom crested and broke, he left for a string of startups. One of them was bought by Motorola, whose stock then halved. The next and the next run by men who hired him to lead their engineering, and then overruled the engineer they had hired. In 2003, he decided he was through taking other people’s orders. He founded Casa and Lucy joined him soon after.

sentences.en: Her father, Jerry Guo, had come to America from Hunan in 1987.
sentences.zh: 她的父亲郭杰瑞于1987年从湖南来到美国。

sentences.en: He was 24 with $50 and a transcript from Tsinghua, having scored first on the gaokao, the exam by which Chinese society sorts its 18-year-olds.
sentences.zh: 24 岁时，他拿着 50 美元和一张清华大学的成绩单，并在高考（中国社会对 18 岁年轻人进行分类的考试）中取得了第一名。

sentences.en: Her mother, Lucy Xie, an engineer, followed a year later.
sentences.zh: 一年后，她的母亲、工程师露西·谢 (Lucy Xie) 也加入了她的行列。

sentences.en: Bell Labs hired them both.
sentences.zh: 贝尔实验室雇用了他们两人。

sentences.en: Jerry could have stayed for life.
sentences.zh: 杰瑞本可以留下来一辈子。

sentences.en: Instead, as the dot-com boom crested and broke, he left for a string of startups.
sentences.zh: 相反，随着互联网泡沫的顶峰和破灭，他离开公司，创办了一系列初创公司。

sentences.en: One of them was bought by Motorola, whose stock then halved.
sentences.zh: 其中一家被摩托罗拉收购，其股价随后减半。

sentences.en: The next and the next run by men who hired him to lead their engineering, and then overruled the engineer they had hired.
sentences.zh: 下一个和下一个由雇用他来领导他们的工程的人领导，然后又否决了他们雇用的工程师。

sentences.en: In 2003, he decided he was through taking other people’s orders.
sentences.zh: 2003年，他决定不再听从别人的命令。

sentences.en: He founded Casa and Lucy joined him soon after.
sentences.zh: 他创立了 Casa，露西很快就加入了他。

blocks.html_en: Early on, a large incumbent sued Casa on what turned out to be no real legal grounds. The case eventually settled for nothing, but the legal fees that year exceeded the company’s revenue. It was at this moment Guo began to absorb what she would later identify as the central fact of company-building, which was that the company always felt like it could die at any moment, that the work was conducted as if by a small band of pirates against Goliath, and that the only available advantages were speed and product and the focus that comes from being always in wartime.

sentences.en: Early on, a large incumbent sued Casa on what turned out to be no real legal grounds.
sentences.zh: 早些时候，一位大公司以没有真正法律依据的理由起诉卡萨。

sentences.en: The case eventually settled for nothing, but the legal fees that year exceeded the company’s revenue.
sentences.zh: 官司最终不了了之，但当年的律师费却超过了公司的收入。

sentences.en: It was at this moment Guo began to absorb what she would later identify as the central fact of company-building, which was that the company always felt like it could die at any moment, that the work was conducted as if by a small band of pirates against Goliath, and that the only available advantages were speed and product and the focus that comes from being always in wartime.
sentences.zh: 正是在这个时候，郭开始吸收她后来认为的公司建设的核心事实，那就是公司总是感觉自己随时可能消亡，工作就像一小群海盗对抗歌利亚一样进行，唯一可用的优势是速度和产品以及始终处于战时的焦点。

blocks.html_en: She loved it.

sentences.en: She loved it.
sentences.zh: 她喜欢它。

blocks.html_en: She had also concluded, somewhere in her teenage years, that she was not going to be the world’s best engineer. In an Instagram exchange in 2022 she said her largest insecurity was that she wasn’t smart enough, which may explain her academic record. She went to Phillips Academy, whose alumni include the Bush presidents and five Nobel laureates, and then to the University of Pennsylvania, where she enrolled in a program called submatriculation, which permits undergraduates to begin graduate work, and then she doubled it. She emerged with four degrees: a BA and MA in Chinese history and literature, a BS in economics, and an MBA. “Very anti-Silicon Valley,” she said. “I did a lot of school.”

sentences.en: She had also concluded, somewhere in her teenage years, that she was not going to be the world’s best engineer.
sentences.zh: 在她十几岁的时候，她还得出结论，她不会成为世界上最好的工程师。

sentences.en: In an Instagram exchange in 2022 she said her largest insecurity was that she wasn’t smart enough, which may explain her academic record.
sentences.zh: 在 2022 年的一次 Instagram 交流中，她表示自己最大的不安全感是自己不够聪明，这或许可以解释她的学习成绩。

sentences.en: She went to Phillips Academy, whose alumni include the Bush presidents and five Nobel laureates, and then to the University of Pennsylvania, where she enrolled in a program called submatriculation, which permits undergraduates to begin graduate work, and then she doubled it.
sentences.zh: 她去了菲利普斯学院，该学院的校友包括布什总统和五位诺贝尔奖获得者，然后去了宾夕法尼亚大学，在那里她参加了一个名为预科入学的项目，该项目允许本科生开始研究生工作，然后她的学位翻了一番。

sentences.en: She emerged with four degrees: a BA and MA in Chinese history and literature, a BS in economics, and an MBA.
sentences.zh: 她获得了四个学位：中国历史和文学学士和硕士学位、经济学学士学位和工商管理硕士学位。

sentences.en: “Very anti-Silicon Valley,” she said.
sentences.zh: “非常反硅谷，”她说。

sentences.en: “I did a lot of school.”
sentences.zh: “我上了很多学校。”

blocks.html_en: She also tried, at Penn, to start two companies in the manner of college students, who really only have two ideas: course catalog management and social dating. Neither worked. None of this resolved the original problem, which was that she had been raised in a house of founders and was looking for a way to become one herself. It did, however, teach her where to go.

sentences.en: She also tried, at Penn, to start two companies in the manner of college students, who really only have two ideas: course catalog management and social dating.
sentences.zh: 她还在宾夕法尼亚大学尝试以大学生的方式创办两家公司，而大学生实际上只有两个想法：课程目录管理和社交约会。

sentences.en: Neither worked.
sentences.zh: 两者都不起作用。

sentences.en: None of this resolved the original problem, which was that she had been raised in a house of founders and was looking for a way to become one herself.
sentences.zh: 所有这些都没有解决最初的问题，即她是在一个创始人之家长大的，并且正在寻找一种方法来成为自己的创始人。

sentences.en: It did, however, teach her where to go.
sentences.zh: 然而，它确实教会了她该去哪里。

blocks.html_en: “It’s the center of the universe,” she said of her decision to move to Silicon Valley after Penn. “Those people are a step ahead. You could characterize some of what I’ve been trying to do for a long time as I just want to get to the center of it, whatever the next thing is.”

sentences.en: “It’s the center of the universe,” she said of her decision to move to Silicon Valley after Penn.
sentences.zh: “这是宇宙的中心，”她在谈到从宾夕法尼亚大学毕业后搬到硅谷的决定时说道。

sentences.en: “Those people are a step ahead.
sentences.zh: “那些人领先一步。

sentences.en: You could characterize some of what I’ve been trying to do for a long time as I just want to get to the center of it, whatever the next thing is.”
sentences.zh: 你可以描述我长期以来一直在尝试做的一些事情，因为我只想到达它的中心，无论接下来的事情是什么。”

blocks.html_en: She moved to San Francisco in 2012 and went to work at Goldman Sachs. She had no particular interest in investing. Her parents’ company had been backed by Summit Partners, the growth-equity firm. She had met those people and the form had not appealed to her. Goldman, she figured, would be a place she could learn why technology businesses are valuable and what makes one especially good. She was clear on what it was not: her life’s work. The business was IPOs and growth-stage advisory. She worked on Workday’s IPO. Her public clients included Netflix and Zynga. And there was Nvidia.

sentences.en: She moved to San Francisco in 2012 and went to work at Goldman Sachs.
sentences.zh: 她于2012年搬到旧金山，并在高盛工作。

sentences.en: She had no particular interest in investing.
sentences.zh: 她对投资没有特别的兴趣。

sentences.en: Her parents’ company had been backed by Summit Partners, the growth-equity firm.
sentences.zh: 她父母的公司得到了成长型股权公司 Summit Partners 的支持。

sentences.en: She had met those people and the form had not appealed to her.
sentences.zh: 她见过那些人，但这种形式对她没有吸引力。

sentences.en: Goldman, she figured, would be a place she could learn why technology businesses are valuable and what makes one especially good.
sentences.zh: 她认为，高盛将是一个让她了解科技企业为何有价值以及为何科技企业特别优秀的地方。

sentences.en: She was clear on what it was not: her life’s work.
sentences.zh: 她很清楚这不是什么：她一生的工作。

sentences.en: The business was IPOs and growth-stage advisory.
sentences.zh: 该业务是首次公开募股和成长期咨询。

sentences.en: She worked on Workday’s IPO.
sentences.zh: 她参与了 Workday 的 IPO。

sentences.en: Her public clients included Netflix and Zynga.
sentences.zh: 她的公共客户包括 Netflix 和 Zynga。

sentences.en: And there was Nvidia.
sentences.zh: 还有英伟达。

blocks.html_en: Nvidia at the time was a $7 billion gaming-chip company. Its stock had been flat for four years. An activist hedge fund called Starboard Value had taken a position; its founder, a man named Jeff Smith, would later replace the entire board of Darden Restaurants over the breadsticks at Olive Garden. Smith wanted Nvidia to return capital to shareholders and stop spending on a side project called CUDA, which Wall Street regarded as a distraction. Goldman’s job was to explain the options to Jensen Huang. He was not interested in giving up CUDA. The buybacks he agreed to. Nvidia announced them in November, and Starboard sold its position the following spring. It was, Guo thought at the time, not an important client. She is fairly sure Huang was wearing a leather jacket.

sentences.en: Nvidia at the time was a $7 billion gaming-chip company.
sentences.zh: 当时的英伟达是一家价值 70 亿美元的游戏芯片公司。

sentences.en: Its stock had been flat for four years.
sentences.zh: 其股价四年来一直持平。
