sentences.en: An activist hedge fund called Starboard Value had taken a position; its founder, a man named Jeff Smith, would later replace the entire board of Darden Restaurants over the breadsticks at Olive Garden.
sentences.zh: 一家名为 Starboard Value 的激进对冲基金已采取行动；它的创始人杰夫·史密斯后来取代了橄榄园面包棒的达顿餐厅的整个董事会。

sentences.en: Smith wanted Nvidia to return capital to shareholders and stop spending on a side project called CUDA, which Wall Street regarded as a distraction.
sentences.zh: 史密斯希望英伟达向股东返还资本，并停止在名为 CUDA 的副业项目上投入资金，华尔街认为该项目是一种干扰。

sentences.en: Goldman’s job was to explain the options to Jensen Huang.
sentences.zh: 高盛的工作是向黄仁勋解释这些选择。

sentences.en: He was not interested in giving up CUDA.
sentences.zh: 他没有兴趣放弃 CUDA。

sentences.en: The buybacks he agreed to.
sentences.zh: 他同意的回购。

sentences.en: Nvidia announced them in November, and Starboard sold its position the following spring.
sentences.zh: Nvidia 在 11 月宣布了这些计划，Starboard 在第二年春天出售了其职位。

sentences.en: It was, Guo thought at the time, not an important client.
sentences.zh: 郭当时认为，这并不是一个重要的客户。

sentences.en: She is fairly sure Huang was wearing a leather jacket.
sentences.zh: 她相当确定黄穿着一件皮夹克。

blocks.html_en: People keep talking about an explosion in entrepreneurship, and I’d say there is a huge amount of opportunity that is absolutely growing. Yet the number of people prepared to really start exceptional companies is not exponential.

sentences.en: People keep talking about an explosion in entrepreneurship, and I’d say there is a huge amount of opportunity that is absolutely growing.
sentences.zh: 人们一直在谈论创业的爆炸式增长，我想说，有大量的机会绝对在增长。

sentences.en: Yet the number of people prepared to really start exceptional companies is not exponential.
sentences.zh: 然而，准备真正创办卓越公司的人数并不是指数级的。

blocks.html_en: People keep talking about an explosion in entrepreneurship, and I’d say there is a huge amount of opportunity that is absolutely growing. Yet the number of people prepared to really start exceptional companies is not exponential.

sentences.en: People keep talking about an explosion in entrepreneurship, and I’d say there is a huge amount of opportunity that is absolutely growing.
sentences.zh: 人们一直在谈论创业的爆炸式增长，我想说，有大量的机会绝对在增长。

sentences.en: Yet the number of people prepared to really start exceptional companies is not exponential.
sentences.zh: 然而，准备真正创办卓越公司的人数并不是指数级的。

blocks.html_en: The client who would change her life was Workday. Workday’s CEO, Aneel Bhusri, had once worked at Morgan Stanley before a man named Dave Duffield pulled him out to be the technology leader he was supposed to be; first at PeopleSoft, and later at Workday. Bhusri seemed to recognize a similar situation in Guo. He tried first to bring her to Workday. She went to their all-hands, met the leaders, and concluded that a company that had just gone public was the wrong stage for someone who wanted “zero to one.” Bhusri, who was also a partner at Greylock Partners, made his second pitch. Stop thinking about joining Stripe or some random company, he told her. She should come to Greylock where she could understand great businesses zero to one, because that’s what Greylock had been doing for some 50 years.

sentences.en: The client who would change her life was Workday.
sentences.zh: 改变她生活的客户是Workday。

sentences.en: Workday’s CEO, Aneel Bhusri, had once worked at Morgan Stanley before a man named Dave Duffield pulled him out to be the technology leader he was supposed to be; first at PeopleSoft, and later at Workday.
sentences.zh: Workday 的首席执行官阿内尔·布斯里 (Aneel Bhusri) 曾在摩根士丹利 (Morgan Stanley) 工作，后来被戴夫·杜菲尔德 (Dave Duffield) 挖走，让他担任技术领导者。首先在 PeopleSoft，后来在 Workday。

sentences.en: Bhusri seemed to recognize a similar situation in Guo.
sentences.zh: 布斯里似乎也意识到了郭的类似情况。

sentences.en: He tried first to bring her to Workday.
sentences.zh: 他首先尝试带她去工作日。

sentences.en: She went to their all-hands, met the leaders, and concluded that a company that had just gone public was the wrong stage for someone who wanted “zero to one.”
sentences.zh: 她召集了全体员工，会见了领导层，得出的结论是，对于那些想要“从零到一”的人来说，刚刚上市的公司是错误的阶段。

sentences.en: Bhusri, who was also a partner at Greylock Partners, made his second pitch.
sentences.zh: 布斯里（Bhusri）也是 Greylock Partners 的合伙人，他进行了第二次推销。

sentences.en: Stop thinking about joining Stripe or some random company, he told her.
sentences.zh: 他告诉她，别再考虑加入 Stripe 或其他随意的公司了。

sentences.en: She should come to Greylock where she could understand great businesses zero to one, because that’s what Greylock had been doing for some 50 years.
sentences.zh: 她应该来格雷洛克，在那里她可以从零到一地了解伟大的企业，因为这就是格雷洛克五十年来一直在做的事情。

blocks.html_en: She agreed to take a meeting and was suitably convinced to leave Goldman after only one year of the two-year analyst program. It annoyed them. But she had no intention of being at Greylock for long either. They had no plan for her, and there was no track to becoming a GP, but most importantly: “I idolized entrepreneurs,” she said. “I wanted to go and start a company. I just needed a better idea.”

sentences.en: She agreed to take a meeting and was suitably convinced to leave Goldman after only one year of the two-year analyst program.
sentences.zh: 她同意参加一次会议，并在为期两年的分析师计划仅一年后就被说服离开高盛。

sentences.en: It annoyed them.
sentences.zh: 这让他们很恼火。

sentences.en: But she had no intention of being at Greylock for long either.
sentences.zh: 但她也不打算在格雷洛克呆太久。

sentences.en: They had no plan for her, and there was no track to becoming a GP, but most importantly: “I idolized entrepreneurs,” she said.
sentences.zh: 他们没有为她制定计划，也没有成为全科医生的途径，但最重要的是：“我崇拜企业家，”她说。

sentences.en: “I wanted to go and start a company.
sentences.zh: “我想去创办一家公司。

sentences.en: I just needed a better idea.”
sentences.zh: 我只是需要一个更好的主意。”

blocks.html_en: It would take her almost a decade. She joined Greylock in 2013, a firm founded in 1965 and nearly as old as the venture business itself, and in the years that followed she helped lead the Series A in Figma, worked on Musical.ly, the app that would become TikTok, helped build a security firm and a customer-support firm, and sat between Reid Hoffman and Asheem Chandna, the firm’s veteran security and infrastructure investor. In 2016 she nearly left to co-found an AI language-learning company with Andrew Ng, the deep-learning pioneer. You could not ask for a better technical co-founder, she told me, and yet the offer only clarified that she was an investor after all. She had her first child, and a few months later, at 28, was made Greylock’s youngest-ever general partner.

sentences.en: It would take her almost a decade.
sentences.zh: 她花了将近十年的时间。

sentences.en: She joined Greylock in 2013, a firm founded in 1965 and nearly as old as the venture business itself, and in the years that followed she helped lead the Series A in Figma, worked on Musical.ly, the app that would become TikTok, helped build a security firm and a customer-support firm, and sat between Reid Hoffman and Asheem Chandna, the firm’s veteran security and infrastructure investor.
sentences.zh: 她于 2013 年加入 Greylock，这家公司成立于 1965 年，几乎与风险投资业务本身一样悠久。在接下来的几年里，她帮助领导了 Figma 的 A 轮融资，参与了 Musical.ly（后来成为 TikTok 的应用程序）的工作，帮助建立了一家安全公司和一家客户支持公司，并担任 Reid Hoffman 和 Asheem Chandna 之间的职务，后者是该公司资深的安全和基础设施投资者。

sentences.en: In 2016 she nearly left to co-found an AI language-learning company with Andrew Ng, the deep-learning pioneer.
sentences.zh: 2016 年，她险些离职，与深度学习先驱吴恩达 (Andrew Ng) 共同创立了一家人工智能语言学习公司。

sentences.en: You could not ask for a better technical co-founder, she told me, and yet the offer only clarified that she was an investor after all.
sentences.zh: 她告诉我，你找不到比这更好的技术联合创始人了，但这一提议只是澄清了她毕竟是一名投资者。

sentences.en: She had her first child, and a few months later, at 28, was made Greylock’s youngest-ever general partner.
sentences.zh: 她生下了第一个孩子，几个月后，28 岁的她成为格雷洛克有史以来最年轻的普通合伙人。

blocks.html_en: Jeffrey Yan turned down $100 million, airdropped billions to strangers, and can’t travel without a bodyguard. The story of how he built Hyperliquid, a blockchain and crypto trading exchange, into the most profitable startup per employee on earth.

sentences.en: Jeffrey Yan turned down $100 million, airdropped billions to strangers, and can’t travel without a bodyguard.
sentences.zh: Jeffrey Yan 拒绝了 1 亿美元，向陌生人空投了数十亿美元，而且没有保镖就不能旅行。

sentences.zh: 讲述他如何将区块链和加密货币交易交易所 Hyperliquid 打造成全球员工人均盈利最高的初创公司的故事。

sentences.en: The Making of a Market Maker
sentences.zh: 做市商的形成

blocks.html_en: How Thomas Peterffy built the machines that killed the trading floor and made Interactive Brokers into a $100 billion business

sentences.en: How Thomas Peterffy built the machines that killed the trading floor and made Interactive Brokers into a $100 billion business
sentences.zh: 托马斯·彼得菲 (Thomas Peterffy) 如何建造消灭交易大厅的机器并使盈透证券 (Interactive Brokers) 成为价值 1000 亿美元的企业

blocks.html_en: Greylock had been good to her, and she credits her partners with “treating me like a partner long before I had the title or experience for it.” She was recruiting GPs before she was one, and once, very young, vetoed a senior hire. But in her latter years she had reached the edge of what she could do from inside someone else’s firm. She had views on whom it should hire, where it should place its bets, how the coming era should be played, and not yet the standing to act on them. “My DPI to Greylock,” she told me, using the venture term for the cash a fund has returned to its investors, “did not command the amount of change I wanted to make. I’m pretty confident I’ll do well by them in the end, but at that moment in time, it was just very unclear.” In June 2022 she resigned.

sentences.en: Greylock had been good to her, and she credits her partners with “treating me like a partner long before I had the title or experience for it.”
sentences.zh: 格雷洛克对她很好，她称赞她的合伙人“早在我获得头衔或经验之前就像合伙人一样对待我。”

sentences.en: She was recruiting GPs before she was one, and once, very young, vetoed a senior hire.
sentences.zh: 她一岁之前就开始招聘全科医生，有一次，她很年轻，就否决了一名高级医生的聘用。

sentences.en: But in her latter years she had reached the edge of what she could do from inside someone else’s firm.
sentences.zh: 但在她的晚年，她已经达到了在别人公司内部所能做的事情的极限。

sentences.en: She had views on whom it should hire, where it should place its bets, how the coming era should be played, and not yet the standing to act on them.
sentences.zh: 她对公司应该雇用谁、应该在哪里下注、如何应对即将到来的时代有自己的看法，但还没有采取行动的资格。

sentences.en: “My DPI to Greylock,” she told me, using the venture term for the cash a fund has returned to its investors, “did not command the amount of change I wanted to make.
sentences.zh: 她告诉我：“我对 Greylock 的 DPI 并没有达到我想要做出的改变的程度。”

sentences.en: I’m pretty confident I’ll do well by them in the end, but at that moment in time, it was just very unclear.”
sentences.zh: 我非常有信心最终我会在他们身上取得好成绩，但在那一刻，一切都还不清楚。”

sentences.en: In June 2022 she resigned.
sentences.zh: 2022年6月，她辞职。

blocks.html_en: By then, her parents’ company had been on its own ride. On December 14, 2017, 14 years after Jerry founded it, Casa Systems began trading on the Nasdaq under the ticker CASA. The implied market cap was $1.2 billion. The man who had arrived from Hunan with $50 had taken his company public at over a billion dollars—built, as Guo said, “with so much love by a bunch of immigrant engineers with very little sophistication about fundraising, marketing, sales, external support,” who had gotten there on the strength of “just solving a problem better.”

sentences.en: By then, her parents’ company had been on its own ride.
sentences.zh: 那时，她父母的公司已经步入正轨。

sentences.en: On December 14, 2017, 14 years after Jerry founded it, Casa Systems began trading on the Nasdaq under the ticker CASA.
sentences.zh: 2017 年 12 月 14 日，即 Jerry 创立 14 年后，Casa Systems 开始在纳斯达克上市，股票代码为 CASA。

sentences.en: The implied market cap was $1.2 billion.
sentences.zh: 隐含市值为 12 亿美元。

sentences.en: The man who had arrived from Hunan with $50 had taken his company public at over a billion dollars—built, as Guo said, “with so much love by a bunch of immigrant engineers with very little sophistication about fundraising, marketing, sales, external support,” who had gotten there on the strength of “just solving a problem better.”
sentences.zh: 这位带着 50 美元从湖南来到这里的人，以超过 10 亿美元的价格将自己的公司上市——正如郭所说，“是由一群移民工程师倾注了极大的爱而建立的，他们对融资、营销、销售和外部支持都不太了解”，而他们的成功是因为“只是更好地解决问题”。

blocks.html_en: The trouble was that public markets demand other things, too. The stock peaked in 2018 and began a long slide. Casa’s second act, becoming a wireless-equipment company after years as a cable-equipment one, worked for a while, then didn’t. In April 2022 Verizon paid $40 million for almost 10% of the company in exchange for a 5G contract, a price that valued Casa at around $400 million, a third of what it had been worth the day it went public. In March 2023, Jerry stepped down as chief executive of the company he had founded 20 years earlier. That August the headquarters in Andover, where Guo had done her homework, sold for $6.4 million. A year later, it was over. Casa filed for Chapter 11.

sentences.en: The trouble was that public markets demand other things, too.
sentences.zh: 问题在于公开市场还需要其他东西。

sentences.en: The stock peaked in 2018 and began a long slide.
sentences.zh: 该股在2018年见顶后开始长期下滑。

sentences.en: Casa’s second act, becoming a wireless-equipment company after years as a cable-equipment one, worked for a while, then didn’t.
sentences.zh: Casa 的第二个举措是，在多年从事有线设备公司之后，成为一家无线设备公司，该公司曾一度奏效，但后来却失败了。

sentences.en: In April 2022 Verizon paid $40 million for almost 10% of the company in exchange for a 5G contract, a price that valued Casa at around $400 million, a third of what it had been worth the day it went public.
sentences.zh: 2022 年 4 月，Verizon 以 4000 万美元收购了该公司近 10% 的股份，以换取一份 5G 合同，这一价格使 Casa 的估值约为 4 亿美元，仅为其上市之日估值的三分之一。

sentences.en: In March 2023, Jerry stepped down as chief executive of the company he had founded 20 years earlier.
sentences.zh: 2023 年 3 月，杰里辞去了他 20 年前创立的公司首席执行官的职务。

sentences.en: That August the headquarters in Andover, where Guo had done her homework, sold for $6.4 million.
sentences.zh: 那年 8 月，郭在安多佛做功课的总部以 640 万美元的价格售出。

sentences.en: A year later, it was over.
sentences.zh: 一年后，一切都结束了。

sentences.en: Casa filed for Chapter 11.
sentences.zh: Casa 申请了美国破产法第 11 章。

blocks.html_en: Two weeks before Embed’s opening weekend, Huang had been in the office. It was Friday, March 6. He had driven up from Santa Clara to hand-deliver Nvidia’s first DGX Station to Karpathy at the Palo Alto office Conviction and Karpathy shared. The DGX was a matte black tower. It looked like the desktop computers people used to buy 20 years ago, except this one cost around $100,000 and was effectively a data center in a box. Across the front, in capitals and gold marker, Huang had written:

sentences.en: Two weeks before Embed’s opening weekend, Huang had been in the office.
sentences.zh: Embed 开业周末前两周，黄已经在办公室了。

sentences.en: It was Friday, March 6.
sentences.zh: 那是 3 月 6 日星期五。

sentences.en: He had driven up from Santa Clara to hand-deliver Nvidia’s first DGX Station to Karpathy at the Palo Alto office Conviction and Karpathy shared.
sentences.zh: 他从圣克拉拉开车过来，在 Conviction 和 Karpathy 共用的帕洛阿尔托办公室将 Nvidia 的第一个 DGX 站亲手交付给 Karpathy。

sentences.en: The DGX was a matte black tower.
sentences.zh: DGX 是一座哑光黑色塔。

sentences.en: It looked like the desktop computers people used to buy 20 years ago, except this one cost around $100,000 and was effectively a data center in a box.
sentences.zh: 它看起来就像人们 20 年前购买的台式电脑，只不过这台电脑售价约为 10 万美元，而且实际上是一个盒子里的数据中心。

sentences.en: Across the front, in capitals and gold marker, Huang had written:
sentences.zh: 在正面，黄用大写字母和金色马克笔写道：

blocks.html_en: TO ANDREJ – THE 1ST DGX STATION. THE AGENTIC ERA OF AI HAS ARRIVED.

sentences.en: TO ANDREJ – THE 1ST DGX STATION.
sentences.zh: 前往 ANDREJ – 第一个 DGX 站。

sentences.en: THE AGENTIC ERA OF AI HAS ARRIVED.
sentences.zh: 人工智能的代理时代已经到来。

blocks.html_en: Two of Huang’s people came along, the founders of a small company called Brev that Nvidia had recently acquired. Also in the office that afternoon were John Hegeman, until recently Meta’s chief revenue officer, and Ravi Gupta, formerly a partner at Sequoia—both now co-founders of a company called Ithaca. Reddy and Garcia-Camargo were around. Someone suggested drinks. The group walked down to Local Union 271.

sentences.en: Two of Huang’s people came along, the founders of a small company called Brev that Nvidia had recently acquired.
sentences.zh: 黄的两个人也来了，他们是英伟达最近收购的一家名为 Brev 的小公司的创始人。

sentences.en: Also in the office that afternoon were John Hegeman, until recently Meta’s chief revenue officer, and Ravi Gupta, formerly a partner at Sequoia—both now co-founders of a company called Ithaca.
sentences.zh: 那天下午也在办公室的还有 Meta 的首席营收官约翰·赫格曼 (John Hegeman) 和红杉资本前合伙人拉维·古普塔 (Ravi Gupta)，两人现在都是一家名为 Ithaca 的公司的联合创始人。

sentences.en: Reddy and Garcia-Camargo were around.
sentences.zh: 雷迪和加西亚-卡马戈就在附近。

sentences.en: Someone suggested drinks.
sentences.zh: 有人建议喝酒。

sentences.en: The group walked down to Local Union 271.
sentences.zh: 一行人走到了 271 号地方工会。

blocks.html_en: Huang stayed five hours, talking about the future, giving advice, and never looked at his phone once. Guo remembers looking at hers three or four times. “Jensen has this wisdom,” she told me, “that if you know what your priorities are and you’re working on the right one, you have all the time in the world. That’s something I aspire to.”

sentences.en: Huang stayed five hours, talking about the future, giving advice, and never looked at his phone once.
sentences.zh: 黄一待了五个小时，谈论未来，给出建议，一次也没有看手机。

sentences.en: Guo remembers looking at hers three or four times.
sentences.zh: 郭记得看了她的三四次。

sentences.en: “Jensen has this wisdom,” she told me, “that if you know what your priorities are and you’re working on the right one, you have all the time in the world.
sentences.zh: “詹森有这样的智慧，”她告诉我，“如果你知道自己的优先事项是什么，并且正在做正确的事情，那么你就拥有了世界上所有的时间。

sentences.en: That’s something I aspire to.”
sentences.zh: 这就是我所渴望的。”

blocks.html_en: Nat Friedman once told her to worry less about being so commercial. “I am very commercial,” she responded. I asked what the intensity had cost her. “The hardest thing is making peace with your own choices,” she said. “Venture is a thing you can pour your whole soul into. I think the answer is, I try to prioritize what I think matters to my kids and then create this environment where they feel like I’m around if they need me.”

sentences.en: Nat Friedman once told her to worry less about being so commercial.
sentences.zh: 纳特·弗里德曼（Nat Friedman）曾经告诉她不要太担心商业化。

sentences.en: “I am very commercial,” she responded.
sentences.zh: “我非常商业化，”她回答道。

sentences.en: I asked what the intensity had cost her.
sentences.zh: 我问这种强度让她付出了怎样的代价。

sentences.en: “The hardest thing is making peace with your own choices,” she said.
sentences.zh: “最困难的事情是接受自己的选择，”她说。

sentences.en: “Venture is a thing you can pour your whole soul into.
sentences.zh: “冒险是一件你可以倾注全部灵魂的事情。

sentences.en: I think the answer is, I try to prioritize what I think matters to my kids and then create this environment where they feel like I’m around if they need me.”
sentences.zh: 我认为答案是，我尝试优先考虑我认为对孩子来说重要的事情，然后创造这样的环境，让他们觉得如果他们需要我，我就在身边。”

blocks.html_en: That she had walked the Met Gala carpet in 45 pounds of chainmail, with her mother beside her, was in keeping. Casa Systems had taught the girl who was worried about not being smart enough that being smart was not enough, and that working every day was not enough either. Companies died anyway. “The existential fear that there are people trying to come kill our companies is part of the way we do venture,” she said. “There’s commitment and loyalty to the team. It’s us against the world.”

sentences.en: That she had walked the Met Gala carpet in 45 pounds of chainmail, with her mother beside her, was in keeping.
sentences.zh: 她穿着 45 磅重的锁子甲走在 Met Gala 地毯上，她的母亲在她身边，这是事实。

sentences.en: Casa Systems had taught the girl who was worried about not being smart enough that being smart was not enough, and that working every day was not enough either.
sentences.zh: 卡萨系统教会了那个担心自己不够聪明的女孩，仅仅聪明是不够的，每天工作也是不够的。

sentences.en: Companies died anyway.
sentences.zh: 无论如何，公司都死了。

sentences.en: “The existential fear that there are people trying to come kill our companies is part of the way we do venture,” she said.
sentences.zh: “对有人试图扼杀我们公司的存在恐惧是我们冒险方式的一部分，”她说。

sentences.en: “There’s commitment and loyalty to the team.
sentences.zh: “对团队有承诺和忠诚。

sentences.en: It’s us against the world.”
sentences.zh: 这是我们对抗世界。”

blocks.html_en: I asked how she thought about the whole arc of her parents’ business, the company built over 20 years from $50 and taken public and then handed to a new CEO and then filed into bankruptcy. She had expected it to crush her parents. It hadn’t. “I find that very reassuring,” she said.

sentences.en: I asked how she thought about the whole arc of her parents’ business, the company built over 20 years from $50 and taken public and then handed to a new CEO and then filed into bankruptcy.
sentences.zh: 我问她如何看待她父母的整个企业，这家公司用了 20 多年的时间从​​ 50 美元建立起来，然后上市，然后交给了​​新的首席执行官，然后申请破产。

sentences.en: She had expected it to crush her parents.
sentences.zh: 她原以为这会压垮她的父母。

sentences.en: It hadn’t.
sentences.zh: 事实并非如此。

sentences.en: “I find that very reassuring,” she said.
sentences.zh: “我觉得这非常令人放心，”她说。

blocks.html_en: “I’ve always told my team, from day zero,” she said. “I want to make this work, but I’m trying to build a partnership. And I don’t want somebody to drag me out of here in a cart. I want the firm to be more interesting and better than I am, because it’s not all of my identity.”

sentences.en: “I’ve always told my team, from day zero,” she said.
sentences.zh: “从第一天起，我就一直告诉我的团队，”她说。

sentences.en: “I want to make this work, but I’m trying to build a partnership.
sentences.zh: “我想让这项工作成功，但我正在努力建立伙伴关系。

sentences.en: And I don’t want somebody to drag me out of here in a cart.
sentences.zh: 我不想有人用推车把我拖出这里。

sentences.en: I want the firm to be more interesting and better than I am, because it’s not all of my identity.”
sentences.zh: 我希望公司比我更有趣、更好，因为这不是我的全部身份。”

blocks.html_en: When Guo left Greylock in June 2022 she had planned to take time off, she told me, to spend time with her family. She got 20 hours into helicopter flying lessons before Conviction’s first fund picked up too much. “As soon as I started talking to LPs,” she said, “I could tell it was happening.” She hasn’t flown since. “Someday I’m going to get back to it, it’ll just have to be after the revolution.”

sentences.en: When Guo left Greylock in June 2022 she had planned to take time off, she told me, to spend time with her family.
sentences.zh: 郭告诉我，当她于 2022 年 6 月离开 Greylock 时，她计划休假，与家人共度时光。

sentences.en: She got 20 hours into helicopter flying lessons before Conviction’s first fund picked up too much.
sentences.zh: 在 Conviction 的第一只基金获得了太多回报之前，她参加了 20 个小时的直升机飞行课程。

sentences.en: “As soon as I started talking to LPs,” she said, “I could tell it was happening.”
sentences.zh: “当我开始与有限合伙人交谈时，”她说，“我就知道这件事正在发生。”

sentences.en: She hasn’t flown since.
sentences.zh: 从那以后她就没有坐过飞机。

sentences.en: “Someday I’m going to get back to it, it’ll just have to be after the revolution.”
sentences.zh: “总有一天我会回到这个话题，但必须是在革命之后。”

blocks.html_en: Grady remembers LPs being somewhat unconvinced by Guo’s pitch for Conviction.

sentences.en: Grady remembers LPs being somewhat unconvinced by Guo’s pitch for Conviction.
sentences.zh: 格雷迪记得有限合伙人对郭的《Conviction》宣传有些不相信。

blocks.html_en: “Every now and then she’d tell me what she was hearing from them,” he said. “The main LP thing was, ‘What’s your strategy? How are you going to win?’ There’s the Patton quote, right? ‘A good plan violently executed now is better than a perfect plan next week.’ And that was her answer. ‘I don’t have a strategy, I’m just going to go win.’ If you’re an LP, that’s not a good pitch. But the reality is there are only so many people in our business who can just go execute, and she’s one of those.”

sentences.en: “Every now and then she’d tell me what she was hearing from them,” he said.
sentences.zh: “她时不时地告诉我她从他们那里听到了什么，”他说。

sentences.en: “The main LP thing was, ‘What’s your strategy?
sentences.zh: “LP 的主要问题是，‘你的策略是什么？

sentences.en: How are you going to win?’
sentences.zh: 你要怎么赢？

sentences.en: There’s the Patton quote, right?
sentences.zh: 这是巴顿的名言，对吗？

sentences.en: ‘A good plan violently executed now is better than a perfect plan next week.’
sentences.zh: “现在暴力执行的好计划比下周的完美计划要好。”

sentences.en: And that was her answer.
sentences.zh: 这就是她的答案。

sentences.en: ‘I don’t have a strategy, I’m just going to go win.’
sentences.zh: “我没有策略，我只想赢。”

sentences.en: If you’re an LP, that’s not a good pitch.
sentences.zh: 如果你是 LP，那不是一个好的推销。

sentences.en: But the reality is there are only so many people in our business who can just go execute, and she’s one of those.”
sentences.zh: 但现实是，我们行业中能够执行的人只有这么多，而她就是其中之一。”

blocks.html_en: Not everyone bought the no-plan story. The fund she’d really wanted to build, some said, was a crypto fund, and the AI thesis was a retrofit. The evidence was not nothing. By the start of 2022, six months before she left, her Twitter avatar changed to a CryptoPunk-style zombie. Around the same time she helped a younger partner lead Greylock’s $70 million Series B into 0x Labs, and shared his board seat. In February she and two Greylock colleagues launched a crypto podcast, Fungible Times. Three days after announcing her departure she told TechCrunch the market was at the start of a new tech cycle, with companies coming across machine learning, AI, data and crypto.

sentences.en: Not everyone bought the no-plan story.
sentences.zh: 并不是所有人都相信这个没有计划的故事。

sentences.en: The fund she’d really wanted to build, some said, was a crypto fund, and the AI thesis was a retrofit.
sentences.zh: 有人说，她真正想建立的基金是加密货币基金，而人工智能论文是一种改造。

sentences.en: The evidence was not nothing.
sentences.zh: 证据并不是什么都没有。

sentences.en: By the start of 2022, six months before she left, her Twitter avatar changed to a CryptoPunk-style zombie.
sentences.zh: 到 2022 年初，也就是她离开的前六个月，她的 Twitter 头像变成了密码朋克风格的僵尸。

sentences.en: Around the same time she helped a younger partner lead Greylock’s $70 million Series B into 0x Labs, and shared his board seat.
sentences.zh: 大约在同一时间，她帮助一位年轻的合伙人领导 Greylock 向 0x Labs 投资 7000 万美元的 B 轮融资，并分享了他的董事会席位。

sentences.en: In February she and two Greylock colleagues launched a crypto podcast, Fungible Times.
sentences.zh: 今年 2 月，她和两位 Greylock 同事推出了加密货币播客 Fungible Times。

sentences.en: Three days after announcing her departure she told TechCrunch the market was at the start of a new tech cycle, with companies coming across machine learning, AI, data and crypto.
sentences.zh: 在宣布离职三天后，她告诉 TechCrunch，市场正处于新科技周期的开始，公司正在涉足机器学习、人工智能、数据和加密货币。

blocks.html_en: By October, when Conviction launched, it was a $100 million fund, branded as “an investing firm for the AI age.” The crypto angle was gone. Five weeks later, FTX went bankrupt. Three weeks after that, ChatGPT shipped.

sentences.en: By October, when Conviction launched, it was a $100 million fund, branded as “an investing firm for the AI age.”
sentences.zh: 到 10 月份，Conviction 推出时，它的规模已达 1 亿美元，被称为“人工智能时代的投资公司”。

sentences.en: The crypto angle was gone.
sentences.zh: 加密货币的角度消失了。

sentences.en: Five weeks later, FTX went bankrupt.
sentences.zh: 五周后，FTX 破产。

sentences.en: Three weeks after that, ChatGPT shipped.
sentences.zh: 三周后，ChatGPT 发货了。

blocks.html_en: “It’s certainly a recurrent rumor,” Guo said. “But I was never going to do a crypto fund.” She had helped run Greylock’s crypto effort and gone as deep in it as she had in AI, the two most interesting ideas in technology in a decade. She is still a personal investor. She still thinks it’s real. What she hadn’t decided, that spring and summer, was whether the firm would be a generalist fund or a single bet. She chose AI because “crypto is fundamentally a financial technology,” she said. “AI is a general technology.” A financial technology, however profound, stays bounded. A general one starts tiny and then, all at once, is everywhere. She wanted to build the next great venture firm, and that lined up with the technology that would be everywhere.

sentences.en: “It’s certainly a recurrent rumor,” Guo said.
sentences.zh: “这肯定是一个反复出现的谣言，”郭说。

sentences.en: “But I was never going to do a crypto fund.”
sentences.zh: “但我永远不会做加密货币基金。”

sentences.en: She had helped run Greylock’s crypto effort and gone as deep in it as she had in AI, the two most interesting ideas in technology in a decade.
sentences.zh: 她帮助运营了 Greylock 的加密货币工作，并像她在人工智能领域一样深入，这是十年来技术领域最有趣的两个想法。

sentences.en: She is still a personal investor.
sentences.zh: 她仍然是个人投资者。

sentences.en: She still thinks it’s real.
sentences.zh: 她仍然认为这是真的。

sentences.en: What she hadn’t decided, that spring and summer, was whether the firm would be a generalist fund or a single bet.
sentences.zh: 那年春天和夏天，她还没有决定该公司是通用型基金还是单一投资基金。

sentences.en: She chose AI because “crypto is fundamentally a financial technology,” she said.
sentences.zh: 她说，她选择人工智能是因为“加密货币本质上是一种金融技术”。

sentences.en: “AI is a general technology.”
sentences.zh: “人工智能是一项通用技术。”

sentences.en: A financial technology, however profound, stays bounded.
sentences.zh: 金融技术无论多么深刻，都是有限度的。

sentences.en: A general one starts tiny and then, all at once, is everywhere.
sentences.zh: 一个通用的开始很小，然后突然变得无处不在。

sentences.en: She wanted to build the next great venture firm, and that lined up with the technology that would be everywhere.
sentences.zh: 她想建立下一个伟大的风险投资公司，并与无处不在的技术相结合。

blocks.html_en: That Guo might have left some people sore is perhaps not surprising. There are wounds still open at Greylock. She’d been there nine years and, near the end, had grown certain the market was changing faster than the firm would. She kept her board seats. She left as well as she knew how. Not all of her former partners have come around. Among them is Asheem Chandna, the senior security and infrastructure investor who had been her mentor.

sentences.en: That Guo might have left some people sore is perhaps not surprising.
sentences.zh: 郭可能让一些人感到痛苦也许并不奇怪。

sentences.en: There are wounds still open at Greylock.
sentences.zh: 格雷洛克的伤口仍然未愈合。

sentences.en: She’d been there nine years and, near the end, had grown certain the market was changing faster than the firm would.
sentences.zh: 她在那里工作了九年，临近年底时，她确信市场的变化速度比公司快。

sentences.en: She kept her board seats.
sentences.zh: 她保留了董事会席位。

sentences.en: She left as well as she knew how.
sentences.zh: 她尽她所能地离开了。

sentences.en: Not all of her former partners have come around.
sentences.zh: 并非所有她以前的合作伙伴都改变了态度。

sentences.en: Among them is Asheem Chandna, the senior security and infrastructure investor who had been her mentor.
sentences.zh: 其中包括曾担任她导师的高级安全和基础设施投资者阿希姆·钱德纳 (Asheem Chandna)。

blocks.html_en: “I’m still very grateful to Asheem,” she said. “I know he’s not pleased I left. I wish I could fix it.”

sentences.en: “I’m still very grateful to Asheem,” she said.
sentences.zh: “我仍然非常感谢阿希姆，”她说。

sentences.en: “I know he’s not pleased I left.
sentences.zh: “我知道他对我离开并不高兴。

sentences.en: I wish I could fix it.”
sentences.zh: 我希望我能修复它。”

blocks.html_en: She has since thought about what it must have looked like from his side. “If one of the people I’d invested in wanted to start from scratch after nine years,” she said, “I’d have to find the space in my heart to think about what’s right for them.”

sentences.en: She has since thought about what it must have looked like from his side.
sentences.zh: 从那时起，她就开始思考从他的角度来看，这会是什么样子。

sentences.en: “If one of the people I’d invested in wanted to start from scratch after nine years,” she said, “I’d have to find the space in my heart to think about what’s right for them.”
sentences.zh: “如果我投资的其中一个人想要在九年后从头开始，”她说，“我就必须在心里找到空间来思考什么对他们来说是正确的。”

blocks.html_en: Guo’s first hire came two weeks after she launched Conviction. Reddy had gone to Penn, like Guo, and like Guo had grown up in the room while an immigrant father built a networking company that ran into the dot-com crash and was sold. He had been the best high-school debater in the country. After college he went to Neeva, the search startup, and was running a third of its engineering when his college roommate, Hunter Lightman, who worked at OpenAI, tried to recruit him over. Reddy said no. OpenAI had 130 people, and a company that size was more than he could imagine working at. It was valued, then, at $29 billion.

sentences.en: Guo’s first hire came two weeks after she launched Conviction.
sentences.zh: 郭在推出 Conviction 两周后迎来了第一个员工。

sentences.en: Reddy had gone to Penn, like Guo, and like Guo had grown up in the room while an immigrant father built a networking company that ran into the dot-com crash and was sold.
sentences.zh: 雷迪和郭一样去了宾夕法尼亚大学，就像郭一样，他是在这个房间里长大的，而他的一位移民父亲创办了一家网络公司，该公司遭遇了互联网泡沫破裂并被出售。

sentences.en: He had been the best high-school debater in the country.
sentences.zh: 他曾是全国最好的高中辩手。

sentences.en: After college he went to Neeva, the search startup, and was running a third of its engineering when his college roommate, Hunter Lightman, who worked at OpenAI, tried to recruit him over.
sentences.zh: 大学毕业后，他去了搜索创业公司 Neeva，负责该公司三分之一的工程工作，当时他的大学室友、在 OpenAI 工作的亨特·莱特曼 (Hunter Lightman) 试图招募他过来。

sentences.en: Reddy said no.
sentences.zh: 雷迪说不。

sentences.en: OpenAI had 130 people, and a company that size was more than he could imagine working at.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 有 130 名员工，公司的规模超出了他的想象。

sentences.en: It was valued, then, at $29 billion.
sentences.zh: 当时它的估值为 290 亿美元。

blocks.html_en: He went to Conviction instead, which at that point was two people, him and Guo. He disputes the idea that backing AI in 2022 made the firm especially visionary. “I think we get some credit, but not that much credit,” he told me. “It was 2022. Other people were earlier than we were. If we win, it’ll be because we were right about the people, not just the thesis.”

sentences.en: He went to Conviction instead, which at that point was two people, him and Guo.
sentences.zh: 他转而去了定罪所，当时有两个人，他和郭。

sentences.en: He disputes the idea that backing AI in 2022 made the firm especially visionary.
sentences.zh: 他对“2022 年支持人工智能使该公司特别有远见”的观点提出质疑。

sentences.en: “I think we get some credit, but not that much credit,” he told me.
sentences.zh: “我认为我们得到了一些荣誉，但没有那么多，”他告诉我。

sentences.en: “It was 2022.
Other people were earlier than we were.
sentences.zh: 其他人比我们早。

sentences.en: If we win, it’ll be because we were right about the people, not just the thesis.”
sentences.zh: 如果我们获胜，那将是因为我们对人的看法是正确的，而不仅仅是论文。”

blocks.html_en: One of Guo’s bigger fears going into Conviction was access. Not just whether a $100 million fund could buy into the companies worth being in, but whether, with Greylock’s name no longer behind her, her calls still got returned. They did. The regret ran the other way. She and Reddy got into the companies but sized the positions, in her word, wimpily.

sentences.en: One of Guo’s bigger fears going into Conviction was access.
sentences.zh: 郭在入狱时更担心的问题之一是接触。

sentences.en: Not just whether a $100 million fund could buy into the companies worth being in, but whether, with Greylock’s name no longer behind her, her calls still got returned.
sentences.zh: 不仅仅是一亿美元的基金是否可以收购那些值得投资的公司，还有，随着格雷洛克的名字不再在她身后，她的电话是否仍然得到回复。

sentences.en: They did.
sentences.zh: 他们做到了。

sentences.en: The regret ran the other way.
sentences.zh: 遗憾却反过来了。

sentences.en: She and Reddy got into the companies but sized the positions, in her word, wimpily.
sentences.zh: 她和雷迪进入了这些公司，但用她的话说，他们对职位的调整却很软弱。

blocks.html_en: The two companies Conviction did not get into were Anthropic and OpenAI. The fund was too small, and they were too big. Yet Guo had backed Baseten in 2019, two years before Anthropic existed, when its founders were still worker ants at OpenAI. The labs grew up around her at Greylock, and she, like most investors, did not catch them. By the spring of 2023, it was too late. OpenAI was taking $10 billion checks from Microsoft and Anthropic’s Series C valued it at over $4 billion.

sentences.en: The two companies Conviction did not get into were Anthropic and OpenAI.
sentences.zh: Conviction 没有进入的两家公司是 Anthropic 和 OpenAI。

sentences.en: The fund was too small, and they were too big.
sentences.zh: 基金太小，而它们又太大。

sentences.en: Yet Guo had backed Baseten in 2019, two years before Anthropic existed, when its founders were still worker ants at OpenAI.
sentences.zh: 然而，郭在 2019 年投资了 Baseten，也就是 Anthropic 出现的两年前，当时其创始人还是 OpenAI 的工蚁。

sentences.en: The labs grew up around her at Greylock, and she, like most investors, did not catch them.
sentences.zh: 格雷洛克的实验室是在她周围长大的，而她和大多数投资者一样，并没有注意到它们。

sentences.en: By the spring of 2023, it was too late.
sentences.zh: 到了 2023 年春天，为时已晚。

sentences.en: OpenAI was taking $10 billion checks from Microsoft and Anthropic’s Series C valued it at over $4 billion.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 从微软获得了 100 亿美元的支票，Anthropic 的 C 轮估值超过 40 亿美元。

blocks.html_en: Guo has come to see the outside as the better place to stand. An investor holding an enormous position in one lab starts reading the whole ecosystem through it, and in a bubble the danger compounds, because people who make money begin to mistake their returns for intelligence. “It’s a fine line,” she said, “between conviction and being dishonest with yourself.” Her distance from the labs is not total. Conviction backed Mistral, Europe’s leading lab, and Thinking Machines, the lab Mira Murati built after leaving OpenAI. What it has never owned is the front of the race. Whether that began as a choice or a consequence hardly matters now; the money is placed, and the revolution is still young. Guo does not agonize over it. “If you ask me, will I take Anthropic stock at the market price? Absolutely I will,” she said. “But it’s not my life goal.”

sentences.en: Guo has come to see the outside as the better place to stand.
sentences.zh: 郭已经开始将外面视为更好的立足之地。

sentences.en: An investor holding an enormous position in one lab starts reading the whole ecosystem through it, and in a bubble the danger compounds, because people who make money begin to mistake their returns for intelligence.
sentences.zh: 在一个实验室中持有巨大头寸的投资者开始通过它来解读整个生态系统，在泡沫中，危险会加剧，因为赚钱的人开始将他们的回报误认为是情报。

sentences.en: “It’s a fine line,” she said, “between conviction and being dishonest with yourself.”
sentences.zh: “信念与对自己不诚实之间只有一线之隔，”她说。

sentences.en: Her distance from the labs is not total.
sentences.zh: 她与实验室的距离并不完全。

sentences.en: Conviction backed Mistral, Europe’s leading lab, and Thinking Machines, the lab Mira Murati built after leaving OpenAI.
sentences.zh: 信念支持欧洲领先的 Mistral 实验室和 Mira Murati 在离开 OpenAI 后建立的 Thinking Machines 实验室。

sentences.en: What it has never owned is the front of the race.
sentences.zh: 它从未拥有过的是比赛的领先优势。

sentences.en: Whether that began as a choice or a consequence hardly matters now; the money is placed, and the revolution is still young.
sentences.zh: 无论这最初是一种选择还是结果，现在已经不重要了；钱已经到位了，革命还很年轻。

sentences.en: Guo does not agonize over it.
sentences.zh: 郭并没有为此苦恼。

sentences.en: “If you ask me, will I take Anthropic stock at the market price?
sentences.zh: “如果你问我，我会以市价买入 Anthropic 股票吗？

sentences.en: Absolutely I will,” she said.
sentences.zh: 我绝对会的，”她说。

sentences.en: “But it’s not my life goal.”
sentences.zh: “但这不是我的人生目标。”

blocks.html_en: She had wanted a peer from the day she started Conviction. As soon as the early bets looked like they might work, and when she saw Vernal was free, she turned it up, in her phrase, to 11. Vernal was not looking for a job. He was 43 and had decided he was finished. He had left Sequoia in 2023, kept his board seats, and spent the next two years picking his kids up from school at 3pm and playing baseball with them. On the side he wrote a dozen or so angel checks into companies like Decagon, Cursor, Cognition, Sunday Robotics, and others that, in his words, “look good on paper.” He called the arrangement idyllic. “Those two years were probably the best years of my life,” he told me.

sentences.en: She had wanted a peer from the day she started Conviction.
sentences.zh: 从她开始定罪的那天起，她就想要一个同伴。

sentences.en: As soon as the early bets looked like they might work, and when she saw Vernal was free, she turned it up, in her phrase, to 11.
sentences.zh: 当早期的赌注看起来可行时，当她看到弗纳尔免费时，她把赌注调到了11，用她的话来说。

sentences.en: Vernal was not looking for a job.
sentences.zh: 弗纳尔并不是在找工作。

sentences.en: He was 43 and had decided he was finished.
sentences.zh: 43 岁的他已经决定自己完了。

sentences.en: He had left Sequoia in 2023, kept his board seats, and spent the next two years picking his kids up from school at 3pm and playing baseball with them.
sentences.zh: 他于 2023 年离开红杉，保留了董事会席位，并在接下来的两年里下午 3 点接孩子放学，并与他们一起打棒球。

sentences.en: On the side he wrote a dozen or so angel checks into companies like Decagon, Cursor, Cognition, Sunday Robotics, and others that, in his words, “look good on paper.”
sentences.zh: 与此同时，他还给 Decagon、Cursor、Cognition、Sunday Robotics 等公司写了十几张天使支票，用他的话说，“纸面上看起来不错”。

sentences.en: He called the arrangement idyllic.
sentences.zh: 他称这种安排是田园诗般的。

sentences.en: “Those two years were probably the best years of my life,” he told me.
sentences.zh: “那两年可能是我一生中最美好的时光，”他告诉我。

blocks.html_en: He had been a partner at Sequoia since 2016, behind the firm’s investments in Statsig, Rippling, Clay, and Notion. Before that he spent eight and a half years at the previous era’s dominant business, Facebook, arriving in 2008 when it was a couple hundred people and leaving as one of its most senior leaders running product and engineering, reporting to Zuckerberg. In 2012 Wired ran his photograph under the headline “How Facebook’s Top Engineer Is Trying to Read Your Mind.”

sentences.en: He had been a partner at Sequoia since 2016, behind the firm’s investments in Statsig, Rippling, Clay, and Notion.
sentences.zh: 自 2016 年以来，他一直是红杉资本的合伙人，支持该公司对 Statsig、Rippling、Clay 和 Notion 的投资。

sentences.en: Before that he spent eight and a half years at the previous era’s dominant business, Facebook, arriving in 2008 when it was a couple hundred people and leaving as one of its most senior leaders running product and engineering, reporting to Zuckerberg.
sentences.zh: 在此之前，他在前一个时代的主导企业 Facebook 工作了八年半，于 2008 年加入，当时该公司只有几百人，离职时担任负责产品和工程的最高层领导之一，向扎克伯格汇报。

sentences.en: In 2012 Wired ran his photograph under the headline “How Facebook’s Top Engineer Is Trying to Read Your Mind.”
sentences.zh: 2012 年，《连线》杂志以“Facebook 的顶级工程师如何读懂你的想法”为标题刊登了他的照片。

blocks.html_en: Guo knew him a little, socially, from Sequoia events she went to with Grady. A month or two into the idyll she took him for coffee. “Very kindly and very generously,” Vernal said, “Sarah was like, ‘Okay, when are you joining?'” Over the next year and a half she drew him in. First: “I’ll give you keycard access to the office. You should just come work out of here when you’re in SF.” Then: “Do you want to come look at this company with me? I’d really value your opinion.” He came in a day a week, then mentored a class of Embed, then led a deal, all before he’d signed any paperwork. He became a general partner in January 2025.

sentences.en: Guo knew him a little, socially, from Sequoia events she went to with Grady.
sentences.zh: 郭在社交方面对他有一点了解，是因为她和格雷迪一起参加了红杉资本的活动。

sentences.en: A month or two into the idyll she took him for coffee.
sentences.zh: 进入田园生活一两个月后，她带他去喝咖啡。

sentences.en: “Very kindly and very generously,” Vernal said, “Sarah was like, ‘Okay, when are you joining?'” Over the next year and a half she drew him in.
sentences.zh: “非常友善和慷慨，”弗纳尔说，“莎拉说，‘好吧，你什么时候加入？’”在接下来的一年半里，她吸引了他。

sentences.en: First: “I’ll give you keycard access to the office.
sentences.zh: 第一：“我会给你钥匙卡进入办公室。

sentences.en: You should just come work out of here when you’re in SF.”
sentences.zh: 当你在旧金山的时候，你就应该离开这里工作。”

sentences.en: Then: “Do you want to come look at this company with me?
sentences.zh: 然后：“你想和我一起去看看这家公司吗？

sentences.en: I’d really value your opinion.”
sentences.zh: 我真的很重视你的意见。”

sentences.en: He came in a day a week, then mentored a class of Embed, then led a deal, all before he’d signed any paperwork.
sentences.zh: 他每周来一天，然后指导 Embed 的一个班级，然后领导一项交易，所有这一切都是在他签署任何文件之前完成的。

sentences.en: He became a general partner in January 2025.
sentences.zh: 他于2025年1月成为普通合伙人。

blocks.html_en: He does not talk himself up. “I’m a much better number two,” he told me. “I don’t want to be the main person. We are building the firm as an equal partnership, but my motivation is to make Sarah and Conviction as successful as possible.”

sentences.en: He does not talk himself up.
sentences.zh: 他不自吹自擂。

sentences.en: “I’m a much better number two,” he told me.
sentences.zh: “我是一个更好的二号人物，”他告诉我。

sentences.en: “I don’t want to be the main person.
sentences.zh: “我不想成为主角。

sentences.en: We are building the firm as an equal partnership, but my motivation is to make Sarah and Conviction as successful as possible.”
sentences.zh: 我们正在将公司打造为平等的合作伙伴关系，但我的动机是让 Sarah 和 Conviction 尽可能成功。”

blocks.html_en: “You’ll never hear Mike say anything lofty about himself,” said Vijaye Raji, who worked under him at Facebook and is now chief technology officer of applications at OpenAI. Bret Taylor, who was at Facebook with him and now runs Sierra, calls him an insane engineer. Parker Conrad tried to hire him to run product at Rippling. Garcia-Camargo, whom Conviction recruited out of Bridgewater the year Vernal arrived, keeps a running list of facts about him: He took the LSAT one weekend, cold, to see how he’d do, and scored in the mid-170s. He did his taxes in Lisp for six years. His favorite subject at a party is Ubiquiti, the maker of networking gear. He set up a data center in his own house, and once described the topology of its switches and racks in such detail, in a cold email to a founder, that the founder later said it was why he took Sequoia’s money.

sentences.en: “You’ll never hear Mike say anything lofty about himself,” said Vijaye Raji, who worked under him at Facebook and is now chief technology officer of applications at OpenAI.
sentences.zh: “你永远不会听到迈克对自己说任何崇高的话，”维贾耶·拉吉 (Vijaye Raji) 说，他曾在 Facebook 手下工作过，现在是 OpenAI 的应用程序首席技术官。

sentences.en: Bret Taylor, who was at Facebook with him and now runs Sierra, calls him an insane engineer.
sentences.zh: 布雷特·泰勒 (Bret Taylor) 曾与他一起在 Facebook 工作，现在负责 Sierra，称他为疯狂的工程师。

sentences.en: Parker Conrad tried to hire him to run product at Rippling.
sentences.zh: 帕克·康拉德 (Parker Conrad) 试图聘请他来负责 Rippling 的产品运营。

sentences.en: Garcia-Camargo, whom Conviction recruited out of Bridgewater the year Vernal arrived, keeps a running list of facts about him: He took the LSAT one weekend, cold, to see how he’d do, and scored in the mid-170s.
sentences.zh: 加西亚-卡马戈（Garcia-Camargo）是 Conviction 在 Vernal 到来的那年从布里奇沃特挖来的，他记录了一份关于他的事实清单：一个周末，他在寒冷的天气里参加了法学院入学考试（LSAT），看看自己的表现如何，结果得分在 170 多岁左右。

sentences.en: He did his taxes in Lisp for six years.
sentences.zh: 他用 Lisp 报税了六年。

sentences.en: His favorite subject at a party is Ubiquiti, the maker of networking gear.
sentences.zh: 他在聚会上最喜欢的话题是网络设备制造商 Ubiquiti。

sentences.en: He set up a data center in his own house, and once described the topology of its switches and racks in such detail, in a cold email to a founder, that the founder later said it was why he took Sequoia’s money.
sentences.zh: 他在自己家里建立了一个数据中心，并曾在一封给创始人的冷酷电子邮件中如此详细地描述了其交换机和机架的拓扑结构，这位创始人后来表示这就是他拿红杉资本的原因。

blocks.html_en: Every insurgency has a general the world can see and one it cannot. Vernal is the second kind.

sentences.en: Every insurgency has a general the world can see and one it cannot.
sentences.zh: 每一个叛乱组织都有一位世界可以看到的将军，也有一位世界看不到的将军。

sentences.en: Vernal is the second kind.
sentences.zh: 春天是第二种。

blocks.html_en: When I met Vernal, he was wearing a black polo and green On running shoes. The shoes matched the green dial and green strap of his Patek Philippe Nautilus. He had a line of Huang’s stuck in his head. He was annoyed by how good it was. “Man, everything is going right for this person,” he told me, smiling.

sentences.en: When I met Vernal, he was wearing a black polo and green On running shoes.
sentences.zh: 当我见到弗纳尔时，他穿着黑色马球衫和绿色跑鞋。

sentences.en: The shoes matched the green dial and green strap of his Patek Philippe Nautilus.
sentences.zh: 这双鞋与他的百达翡丽鹦鹉螺的绿色表盘和绿色表带相匹配。

sentences.en: He had a line of Huang’s stuck in his head.
sentences.zh: 他的脑海里浮现出黄氏的一句台词。

sentences.en: He was annoyed by how good it was.
sentences.zh: 他对它有多好感到恼火。

sentences.en: “Man, everything is going right for this person,” he told me, smiling.
sentences.zh: “伙计，这个人一切都很顺利，”他微笑着告诉我。

blocks.html_en: Huang had said the addressable market for tech companies used to be the software budget inside other companies. Now, with both large language models and robotics, the addressable market was essentially all of OpEx and all of CapEx. “A lot of doing investing well right now,” Vernal said, “is trying to imagine how big something can get. The size of outcomes here is just so much larger than people realize.”

sentences.en: Huang had said the addressable market for tech companies used to be the software budget inside other companies.
sentences.zh: 黄曾表示，科技公司的潜在市场曾经是其他公司内部的软件预算。

sentences.en: Now, with both large language models and robotics, the addressable market was essentially all of OpEx and all of CapEx.
sentences.zh: 现在，有了大型语言模型和机器人技术，潜在市场基本上就是所有运营支出和所有资本支出。

sentences.en: “A lot of doing investing well right now,” Vernal said, “is trying to imagine how big something can get.
sentences.zh: “现在很多投资做得很好，”弗纳尔说，“就是试图想象某件事能发展到多大。

sentences.en: The size of outcomes here is just so much larger than people realize.”
sentences.zh: 这里的成果规模比人们意识到的要大得多。”

blocks.html_en: The example he reached for was OpenEvidence, whose co-founder and chief technology officer, Zachary Ziegler, he interviewed on Saturday morning in front of the Embed founders at their York Street office.

sentences.en: The example he reached for was OpenEvidence, whose co-founder and chief technology officer, Zachary Ziegler, he interviewed on Saturday morning in front of the Embed founders at their York Street office.
sentences.zh: 他举的例子是 OpenEvidence，周六早上，他在 Embed 约克街办公室的创始人面前接受了 OpenEvidence 的联合创始人兼首席技术官扎卡里·齐格勒 (Zachary Ziegler) 的采访。

blocks.html_en: OpenEvidence is a chatbot for doctors. A physician asks a clinical question, or enters the facts of the patient across the desk, and it answers from the medical literature, the guidelines, the drug labels, 35 million papers in all, with citations attached so the doctor can check the work. Which is to say it does, for doctors, what Claude and ChatGPT already do for everyone, search and answer, and on paper that should be fatal. Yet, it is the fastest-growing application for physicians in history. In April the company said about 65% of American doctors were using it. Aside from the iPhone, nothing in the history of medicine had reached them faster. More than 100 million Americans were treated last year by a doctor who had consulted OpenEvidence.

sentences.en: OpenEvidence is a chatbot for doctors.
sentences.zh: OpenEvidence 是一个医生聊天机器人。

sentences.en: A physician asks a clinical question, or enters the facts of the patient across the desk, and it answers from the medical literature, the guidelines, the drug labels, 35 million papers in all, with citations attached so the doctor can check the work.
sentences.zh: 医生提出一个临床问题，或者在办公桌上输入患者的情况，然后根据医学文献、指南、药物标签以及总共 3500 万篇论文进行回答，并附上引文，以便医生检查工作。

sentences.en: Which is to say it does, for doctors, what Claude and ChatGPT already do for everyone, search and answer, and on paper that should be fatal.
sentences.zh: 也就是说，对于医生来说，Claude 和 ChatGPT 已经为每个人做了同样的事情：搜索和回答，在纸面上这应该是致命的。

sentences.en: Yet, it is the fastest-growing application for physicians in history.
sentences.zh: 然而，它是历史上增长最快的医生应用程序。

sentences.en: In April the company said about 65% of American doctors were using it.
sentences.zh: 4 月份该公司表示，大约 65% 的美国医生正在使用它。

sentences.en: Aside from the iPhone, nothing in the history of medicine had reached them faster.
sentences.zh: 除了 iPhone 之外，医学史上没有任何东西能够更快地到达他们的手中。

sentences.en: More than 100 million Americans were treated last year by a doctor who had consulted OpenEvidence.
sentences.zh: 去年，有超过 1 亿美国人接受了一位咨询过 OpenEvidence 的医生的治疗。

blocks.html_en: The company is barely five years old, founded in late 2021 by Ziegler and Daniel Nadler. Nadler had founded an AI business called Kensho previously. In 2018, he sold it to S&P Global for $550 million. At the time, it was the largest sale of an AI company there had ever been.

sentences.en: The company is barely five years old, founded in late 2021 by Ziegler and Daniel Nadler.
sentences.zh: 该公司成立仅五年，由 Ziegler 和 Daniel Nadler 于 2021 年底创立。

sentences.en: Nadler had founded an AI business called Kensho previously.
sentences.zh: 纳德勒此前曾创立过一家名为 Kensho 的人工智能公司。

sentences.en: In 2018, he sold it to S&P Global for $550 million.
sentences.zh: 2018年，他以5.5亿美元的价格将其出售给S&P Global。

sentences.en: At the time, it was the largest sale of an AI company there had ever been.
sentences.zh: 当时，这是人工智能公司有史以来规模最大的一次出售。

blocks.html_en: Guo and Vernal took Nadler to lunch in the first half of 2025. The Series B was coming together at $3.5 billion against revenue in the single-digit millions, a multiple of something like 1,000. While Nadler talked, Vernal ran the arithmetic he is known for around the firm. Ask him about a women’s textile company in India and he will study the ceiling for a moment and give you, within 10%, the revenue, the market cap, the EBITDA, the gross margin. He has done it since Sequoia. Now he was doing it on OpenEvidence.

sentences.en: Guo and Vernal took Nadler to lunch in the first half of 2025.
sentences.zh: 2025 年上半年，郭和弗纳尔带纳德勒共进午餐。

sentences.en: The Series B was coming together at $3.5 billion against revenue in the single-digit millions, a multiple of something like 1,000.
sentences.zh: B 轮融资总计 35 亿美元，营收为数百万个位数，是 1,000 倍左右。

sentences.en: While Nadler talked, Vernal ran the arithmetic he is known for around the firm.
sentences.zh: 在纳德勒讲话的同时，弗纳尔进行了他在公司内闻名的算术运算。

sentences.en: Ask him about a women’s textile company in India and he will study the ceiling for a moment and give you, within 10%, the revenue, the market cap, the EBITDA, the gross margin.
sentences.zh: 如果你问他关于印度一家女装纺织公司的情况，他会研究一下上限，然后给你 10% 以内的收入、市值、息税折旧摊销前利润 (EBITDA) 和毛利率。

sentences.en: He has done it since Sequoia.
sentences.zh: 自红杉以来他就这么做了。

sentences.en: Now he was doing it on OpenEvidence.
sentences.zh: 现在他在 OpenEvidence 上做这件事。

blocks.html_en: Healthcare is about 20% of American GDP, he thought. Pharma is about four. What pharma spends advertising to doctors, on the back of an envelope, comes to something like 20 basis points of GDP, which is an enormous number, and what OpenEvidence captured was the doctor at the precise moment the patient was describing the symptoms.

sentences.en: Healthcare is about 20% of American GDP, he thought.
sentences.zh: 他认为，医疗保健约占美国 GDP 的 20%。

sentences.en: Pharma is about four.
sentences.zh: 制药业大约是四岁。

sentences.en: What pharma spends advertising to doctors, on the back of an envelope, comes to something like 20 basis points of GDP, which is an enormous number, and what OpenEvidence captured was the doctor at the precise moment the patient was describing the symptoms.
sentences.zh: 制药公司在信封背面向医生做广告的花费约为 GDP 的 20 个基点，这是一个巨大的数字，而 OpenEvidence 捕捉到的正是患者描述症状时的医生。

blocks.html_en: “That is literally the highest-value inventory I could possibly imagine,” Vernal told me. “It’s similar to Google Search.” The only public comparison was Doximity, worth $10 to $15 billion, and for the math to work Vernal had to believe OpenEvidence would run well past it. “My reaction,” he said, “having worked on ads at Facebook for a very long time, was, oh, this can be absolutely ginormous.”

sentences.en: “That is literally the highest-value inventory I could possibly imagine,” Vernal told me.
sentences.zh: “这确实是我能想象到的最高价值的库存，”弗纳尔告诉我。

sentences.en: “It’s similar to Google Search.”
sentences.zh: “它类似于谷歌搜索。”

sentences.en: The only public comparison was Doximity, worth $10 to $15 billion, and for the math to work Vernal had to believe OpenEvidence would run well past it.
sentences.zh: 唯一的公开比较是 Doximity，价值 10 到 150 亿美元，为了让数学发挥作用，Vernal 必须相信 OpenEvidence 能够远远超过它。

sentences.en: “My reaction,” he said, “having worked on ads at Facebook for a very long time, was, oh, this can be absolutely ginormous.”
sentences.zh: 他说：“我在 Facebook 从事广告工作很长时间了，我的反应是，哦，这绝对是巨大的。”

blocks.html_en: Guo and Vernal didn’t need a second meeting. Inside a week they had committed, at $3.5 billion—only the second time they’d invested above a billion dollars. Six months later OpenEvidence raised again at $12 billion, one of the fastest companies ever to reach $100 million in annual revenue.

sentences.en: Guo and Vernal didn’t need a second meeting.
sentences.zh: 郭和弗纳尔不需要第二次会面。

sentences.en: Inside a week they had committed, at $3.5 billion—only the second time they’d invested above a billion dollars.
sentences.zh: 一周之内，他们就承诺投资 35 亿美元——这是他们第二次投资超过 10 亿美元。

sentences.en: Six months later OpenEvidence raised again at $12 billion, one of the fastest companies ever to reach $100 million in annual revenue.
sentences.zh: 六个月后，OpenEvidence 再次融资 120 亿美元，成为年收入达到 1 亿美元最快的公司之一。

blocks.html_en: Guo began to absorb what she would later identify as the central fact of company-building, which was that the company always felt like it could die at any moment, that the work was conducted as if by a small band of pirates against Goliath, and that the only available advantages were speed and product and the focus that comes from being always in wartime.

sentences.en: Guo began to absorb what she would later identify as the central fact of company-building, which was that the company always felt like it could die at any moment, that the work was conducted as if by a small band of pirates against Goliath, and that the only available advantages were speed and product and the focus that comes from being always in wartime.
sentences.zh: 郭开始吸收她后来认为的公司建设的核心事实，那就是公司总是感觉自己随时可能消亡，工作就像一小群海盗对抗歌利亚一样进行，唯一可用的优势是速度和产品以及始终处于战时的焦点。

blocks.html_en: Guo began to absorb what she would later identify as the central fact of company-building, which was that the company always felt like it could die at any moment, that the work was conducted as if by a small band of pirates against Goliath, and that the only available advantages were speed and product and the focus that comes from being always in wartime.

sentences.en: Guo began to absorb what she would later identify as the central fact of company-building, which was that the company always felt like it could die at any moment, that the work was conducted as if by a small band of pirates against Goliath, and that the only available advantages were speed and product and the focus that comes from being always in wartime.
sentences.zh: 郭开始吸收她后来认为的公司建设的核心事实，那就是公司总是感觉自己随时可能消亡，工作就像一小群海盗对抗歌利亚一样进行，唯一可用的优势是速度和产品以及始终处于战时的焦点。

blocks.html_en: When Vernal introduced Ziegler to the Embed class, he told them OpenEvidence was one example of a $100 million run-rate business that venture had previously given up on. Harvey and Sierra were two others. “I think health tech still sucks, man,” Guo told me. “OpenEvidence is a very specific type of company started by a really special person.”

sentences.en: When Vernal introduced Ziegler to the Embed class, he told them OpenEvidence was one example of a $100 million run-rate business that venture had previously given up on.
sentences.zh: 当 Vernal 将 Ziegler 介绍给 Embed 班级时，他告诉他们 OpenEvidence 是风险投资公司此前放弃的价值 1 亿美元的运行率业务的一个例子。

sentences.en: Harvey and Sierra were two others.
sentences.zh: 哈维和塞拉是另外两个人。

sentences.en: “I think health tech still sucks, man,” Guo told me.
sentences.zh: “我认为健康科技仍然很糟糕，伙计，”郭告诉我。

sentences.en: “OpenEvidence is a very specific type of company started by a really special person.”
sentences.zh: “OpenEvidence 是一家非常特殊的公司，由一位非常特别的人创办。”

blocks.html_en: “People keep talking about an explosion in entrepreneurship,” she said. “And I’d say there is a huge amount of opportunity that is absolutely growing. And yet the number of people prepared to really start exceptional companies is not exponential.”

sentences.en: “People keep talking about an explosion in entrepreneurship,” she said.
sentences.zh: “人们一直在谈论创业精神的爆炸式增长，”她说。

sentences.en: “And I’d say there is a huge amount of opportunity that is absolutely growing.
sentences.zh: “我想说，有大量的机会绝对在增长。

sentences.en: And yet the number of people prepared to really start exceptional companies is not exponential.”
sentences.zh: 然而，准备真正创办卓越公司的人数并不是指数级的。”

blocks.html_en: Later on that Saturday at Guo’s house down the Peninsula, as the sun fell, the tacos came out. They were served on the chessboard. The kings and queens and pawns were lifted aside, and the squares between the drive and the pool turned into a buffet line. By then Guo’s garden held more than the Embed founders. People had come from Thinking Machines and Cognition and Paradigm, senior people, and they moved among the 18- and 20-year-olds with plates in their hands, Karpathy and Vernal and Garcia-Camargo and Reddy somewhere in the crowd. The temperature dropped. Guo came out of the house in a purple knit sweater, rows of small white sheep marching across it, each row facing the opposite way to the one above. One sheep, over her right collarbone, was black.

sentences.en: Later on that Saturday at Guo’s house down the Peninsula, as the sun fell, the tacos came out.
sentences.zh: 那个周六晚些时候，在半岛的郭家，太阳落山时，炸玉米饼就出来了。

sentences.en: They were served on the chessboard.
sentences.zh: 他们被放在棋盘上。

sentences.en: The kings and queens and pawns were lifted aside, and the squares between the drive and the pool turned into a buffet line.
sentences.zh: 国王、王后和棋子被移到一边，车道和泳池之间的广场变成了自助餐队列。

sentences.en: By then Guo’s garden held more than the Embed founders.
sentences.zh: 到那时，郭的花园里的人比 Embed 创始人还要多。

sentences.en: People had come from Thinking Machines and Cognition and Paradigm, senior people, and they moved among the 18- and 20-year-olds with plates in their hands, Karpathy and Vernal and Garcia-Camargo and Reddy somewhere in the crowd.
sentences.zh: 人们来自思维机器、认知和范式，资深人士，他们在手里拿着盘子的 18 岁和 20 岁的年轻人中移动，卡帕西、弗纳尔、加西亚-卡马戈和雷迪在人群中的某个地方。

sentences.en: The temperature dropped.
sentences.zh: 温度下降了。

sentences.en: Guo came out of the house in a purple knit sweater, rows of small white sheep marching across it, each row facing the opposite way to the one above.
sentences.zh: 郭穿着一件紫色的针织毛衣从房子里出来，一排排小白羊在里面游行，每一排都与上面那排相反。

sentences.en: One sheep, over her right collarbone, was black.
sentences.zh: 一只羊的右锁骨上方是黑色的。

blocks.html_en: She bounced through the garden, talking hires, M&A, schooling, films, silicon. The slang came with her, “sick,” “my guy,” and then, with no gear change at all, the whole banker’s alphabet, dispensed to the twenty-somethings around her. I left her to it a little after 10pm.

sentences.en: She bounced through the garden, talking hires, M&A, schooling, films, silicon.
sentences.zh: 她在花园里蹦蹦跳跳，谈论招聘、并购、学校教育、电影、硅。

sentences.en: The slang came with her, “sick,” “my guy,” and then, with no gear change at all, the whole banker’s alphabet, dispensed to the twenty-somethings around her.
sentences.zh: 俚语伴随着她而来，“生病了”，“我的家伙”，然后，根本没有换档，整个银行家的字母表，分配给了她周围的二十多岁的人。

sentences.en: I left her to it a little after 10pm.
sentences.zh: 晚上 10 点刚过，我就把她留给了她。

blocks.html_en: The next morning the team was back at York Street early. Guo and Vernal sat in front of the founders talking about how to raise money. Guo wore a red baseball cap and a t-shirt that read “Please don’t come to Mars.” Andrew Milich, who ran product engineering at Cursor, had been on the schedule to speak. He did not come. That week he had left for Musk’s xAI. A month later Musk announced he had bought an option to acquire Cursor outright for $60 billion.

sentences.en: The next morning the team was back at York Street early.
sentences.zh: 第二天早上，团队一早回到约克街。

sentences.en: Guo and Vernal sat in front of the founders talking about how to raise money.
sentences.zh: 郭和弗纳尔坐在创始人面前讨论如何筹集资金。

sentences.en: Guo wore a red baseball cap and a t-shirt that read “Please don’t come to Mars.”
sentences.zh: 郭戴着红色棒球帽，身穿印有“请不要来火星”的T恤。

sentences.en: Andrew Milich, who ran product engineering at Cursor, had been on the schedule to speak.
sentences.zh: Cursor 产品工程主管安德鲁·米利奇 (Andrew Milich) 按计划发表讲话。

sentences.en: He did not come.
sentences.zh: 他没有来。

sentences.en: That week he had left for Musk’s xAI.
sentences.zh: 那周他去了马斯克的 xAI。

sentences.en: A month later Musk announced he had bought an option to acquire Cursor outright for $60 billion.
sentences.zh: 一个月后，马斯克宣布他购买了以 600 亿美元彻底收购 Cursor 的选择权。

blocks.html_en: I returned to Conviction at the end of May. It was another clean, bright San Francisco day, and as a Waymo carried me toward the office the news came through, on X first, that Andrej Karpathy had joined Anthropic. He was now a worker ant.

sentences.en: I returned to Conviction at the end of May.
sentences.zh: 我于五月底回到定罪区。

sentences.en: It was another clean, bright San Francisco day, and as a Waymo carried me toward the office the news came through, on X first, that Andrej Karpathy had joined Anthropic.
sentences.zh: 这是旧金山又一个干净、明亮的一天，当 Waymo 载着我去办公室时，X 日第一天传来安德烈·卡帕西 (Andrej Karpathy) 加入 Anthropic 的消息。

sentences.en: He was now a worker ant.
sentences.zh: 他现在是一只工蚁了。

blocks.html_en: Guo had told me once that she knew her father was stressed when she came down in the morning and found the furniture rearranged. I stepped into the office and everything was where I remembered it. The pull-up bar, the bowl of chalk beneath it, the robot dog folded under the whiteboard. Garcia-Camargo was already at her desk. Vernal arrived five minutes after me, Guo five minutes after him. On her desk was a box with a gift inside. It was a black Harvey babygrow. They immediately began talking about “Andrej.” Guo’s phone had been going off.

sentences.en: Guo had told me once that she knew her father was stressed when she came down in the morning and found the furniture rearranged.
sentences.zh: 郭有一次告诉我，当她早上下来发现家具被重新摆放时，她知道父亲压力很大。

sentences.en: I stepped into the office and everything was where I remembered it.
sentences.zh: 我走进办公室，一切都在我记忆中的地方。

sentences.en: The pull-up bar, the bowl of chalk beneath it, the robot dog folded under the whiteboard.
sentences.zh: 引体向上杆，下面有一碗粉笔，机器狗折叠在白板下面。

sentences.en: Garcia-Camargo was already at her desk.
sentences.zh: 加西亚-卡马戈已经坐在她的办公桌前。

sentences.en: Vernal arrived five minutes after me, Guo five minutes after him.
sentences.zh: 弗纳尔比我晚五分钟到达，郭比他晚五分钟到达。

sentences.en: On her desk was a box with a gift inside.
sentences.zh: 她的桌子上放着一个盒子，里面装着一份礼物。

sentences.en: It was a black Harvey babygrow.
sentences.zh: 这是一件黑色的哈维婴儿服。

sentences.en: They immediately began talking about “Andrej.”
sentences.zh: 他们立即开始谈论“安德烈”。

sentences.en: Guo’s phone had been going off.
sentences.zh: 郭的电话一直没电。

blocks.html_en: In the month that followed, two more went. Noam Shazeer, who co-led Google’s Gemini and had co-written the 2017 paper that started all of it, left for OpenAI. Google had paid $2.7 billion to bring him back from a startup barely two years before. He left anyway. John Jumper, who had won a Nobel Prize for teaching a machine to predict the shapes of proteins, left Google’s DeepMind for Anthropic. Both men were leaving Google’s own labs, which had a frontier model and every advantage money buys, for the labs they judged to be ahead of it. By then SpaceX had exercised its option on Cursor. The trillion-dollar firm that owned xAI now also owned the coding agent that, until very recently, had been one of the few that worked.

sentences.en: In the month that followed, two more went.
sentences.zh: 在接下来的一个月里，又有两个人去了。

sentences.en: Noam Shazeer, who co-led Google’s Gemini and had co-written the 2017 paper that started all of it, left for OpenAI.
sentences.zh: 诺姆·沙泽尔 (Noam Shazeer) 离开谷歌 Gemini，并与人共同撰写了 2017 年的论文，该论文开创了这一切，现在他离开了 OpenAI。

sentences.en: Google had paid $2.7 billion to bring him back from a startup barely two years before.
sentences.zh: 就在两年前，谷歌斥资 27 亿美元把他从一家初创公司挖了回来。

sentences.en: He left anyway.
sentences.zh: 他还是离开了。

sentences.en: John Jumper, who had won a Nobel Prize for teaching a machine to predict the shapes of proteins, left Google’s DeepMind for Anthropic.
sentences.zh: 因教授机器预测蛋白质形状而获得诺贝尔奖的约翰·詹珀 (John Jumper) 离开了谷歌的 DeepMind，加入了 Anthropic。

sentences.en: Both men were leaving Google’s own labs, which had a frontier model and every advantage money buys, for the labs they judged to be ahead of it.
sentences.zh: 两人都离开了谷歌自己的实验室，该实验室拥有前沿模型和金钱可以买到的一切优势，前往他们认为领先的实验室。

sentences.en: By then SpaceX had exercised its option on Cursor.
sentences.zh: 那时 SpaceX 已经行使了 Cursor 的选择权。

sentences.en: The trillion-dollar firm that owned xAI now also owned the coding agent that, until very recently, had been one of the few that worked.
sentences.zh: 拥有 xAI 的价值数万亿美元的公司现在也拥有编码代理，直到最近，该代理还是少数有效的编码代理之一。

blocks.html_en: Anthropic, OpenAI, xAI. They were the same names money had been pouring into since January, and now the people were going too, everything bending toward two or three names as if the whole field had begun to fall inward and pick up speed. At least the labs, for now, still need humans.

sentences.en: Anthropic, OpenAI, xAI.
sentences.zh: 人类、OpenAI、xAI。

sentences.en: They were the same names money had been pouring into since January, and now the people were going too, everything bending toward two or three names as if the whole field had begun to fall inward and pick up speed.
sentences.zh: 这些名字是自一月份以来资金不断涌入的名字，现在人们也开始了，一切都向两三个名字倾斜，仿佛整个领域开始向内倾斜并加速。

sentences.en: At least the labs, for now, still need humans.
sentences.zh: 至少目前实验室仍然需要人类。

blocks.html_en: Even inside Conviction the mood had crept in. The question of what was left for early-stage investors, when the labs had so much money, compute, and talent, had become a debate inside the firm. For a stretch the honest answer seemed to be nothing. Guo took the other side. The labs would be enormous, she agreed. But it was not the labs that had built Harvey or Sierra or OpenEvidence. Someone still had to wedge the agents into the doctor’s office, the court filing, the customer-service queue, the accounting close, or the manufacturing floor. Her mother had told her years ago: “Don’t write code, Sarah. Product was not the hard part. People and sales management is the hard part.”

sentences.en: Even inside Conviction the mood had crept in.
sentences.zh: 即使在定罪内部，这种情绪也已悄然蔓延。

sentences.en: The question of what was left for early-stage investors, when the labs had so much money, compute, and talent, had become a debate inside the firm.
sentences.zh: 当实验室拥有如此多的资金、计算能力和人才时，早期投资者还能剩下什么，这个问题已经成为公司内部的争论。

sentences.en: For a stretch the honest answer seemed to be nothing.
sentences.zh: 有一段时间，诚实的回答似乎什么也没有。

sentences.en: Guo took the other side.
sentences.zh: 郭则站在另一边。

sentences.en: The labs would be enormous, she agreed.
sentences.zh: 她同意，实验室会很大。

sentences.en: But it was not the labs that had built Harvey or Sierra or OpenEvidence.
sentences.zh: 但建造 Harvey、Sierra 或 OpenEvidence 的并不是这些实验室。

sentences.en: Someone still had to wedge the agents into the doctor’s office, the court filing, the customer-service queue, the accounting close, or the manufacturing floor.
sentences.zh: 仍然需要有人将特工塞进医生办公室、法庭文件、客户服务队列、会计结算或生产车间。

sentences.en: Her mother had told her years ago: “Don’t write code, Sarah.
sentences.zh: 她的母亲多年前告诉她：“不要写代码，莎拉。

sentences.en: Product was not the hard part.
sentences.zh: 产品并不是最难的部分。

sentences.en: People and sales management is the hard part.”
sentences.zh: 人员和销售管理是困难的部分。”

blocks.html_en: No one can tell you whether any of this ends in extinction or a golden age. The labs keep pulling people and companies out of Conviction’s world, and its world keeps growing anyway. “We have two handfuls of companies that are really working,” she told me. In the eight weeks between my two visits, five of Conviction’s companies raised more money. They are hungrier for cash than the software businesses of the last era ever were, and the private markets can only carry that so far.

sentences.en: No one can tell you whether any of this ends in extinction or a golden age.
sentences.zh: 没有人能告诉你这是否会导致灭绝或黄金时代。

sentences.en: The labs keep pulling people and companies out of Conviction’s world, and its world keeps growing anyway.
sentences.zh: 实验室不断地将人和公司从《Conviction》的世界中拉出来，而它的世界无论如何都在不断发展。

sentences.en: “We have two handfuls of companies that are really working,” she told me.
sentences.zh: “我们有两家真正在运作的公司，”她告诉我。

sentences.en: In the eight weeks between my two visits, five of Conviction’s companies raised more money.
sentences.zh: 在我两次访问之间的八周内，Conviction 旗下的五家公司筹集了更多资金。

sentences.en: They are hungrier for cash than the software businesses of the last era ever were, and the private markets can only carry that so far.
sentences.zh: 他们比上一个时代的软件企业更渴望现金，而私人市场只能支撑到目前为止。

blocks.html_en: “Everything to this point just feels like the prologue to me,” Reddy said. “We haven’t had the big winning company IPO yet, the thing that’s a marker.” When the first startup rings the bell it will be that moment, the proof that Guo and her team are winning their wager. It will also be the day her companies pass out of her hands to investors who have never met her founders.

sentences.en: “Everything to this point just feels like the prologue to me,” Reddy said.
sentences.zh: “到目前为止，一切对我来说都像是序幕，”雷迪说。

sentences.en: “We haven’t had the big winning company IPO yet, the thing that’s a marker.”
sentences.zh: “我们还没有大赢家公司首次公开募股，这是一个标志。”

sentences.en: When the first startup rings the bell it will be that moment, the proof that Guo and her team are winning their wager.
sentences.zh: 当第一家初创公司敲响钟声的那一刻，郭和她的团队就赢得了赌注。

sentences.en: It will also be the day her companies pass out of her hands to investors who have never met her founders.
sentences.zh: 这也将是她的公司从她手中移交给从未见过她的创始人的投资者的那一天。

blocks.html_en: “As somebody who is the daughter of entrepreneurs who took a company public, I loathe the idea,” she said. “And yet we will do it.”

sentences.en: “As somebody who is the daughter of entrepreneurs who took a company public, I loathe the idea,” she said.
sentences.zh: “作为将公司上市的企业家的女儿，我讨厌这个想法，”她说。

sentences.en: “And yet we will do it.”
sentences.zh: “但我们还是会这么做。”

blocks.html_en: Dom Cooke is the managing editor of Colossus.

sentences.en: Dom Cooke is the managing editor of Colossus.
sentences.zh: Dom Cooke 是 Colossus 的总编辑。

content_html_zh:
收听

收听：

2025 年 11 月，Anthropic（其员工称为 Ant）在液冷处理器仓库上训练了一个名为 Claude Opus 4.5 的模型，在工蚁发布该模型的当天，机器就变得具有代理性。 也就是说，他们不再需要牵手。 11 月之前，这些机器感觉就像八岁孩子一样：热切、字面意思、完成你的句子、几乎正确但大部分都是错误的，就像八岁孩子所做的那样。 一夜之间，他们就28岁了。

如果您编写代码，您现在可以用简单的英语告诉他们您想要做什么，然后代理就会去做。 你不必说去哪里寻找，或者如何解决问题，或者首先尝试什么；你描述了完成的东西，它就在那里。 它没有争辩，没有叹息，也没有询问周一是否可以绕回来。 它不会累，不会饿，不会无聊，不会结婚，也不会厌倦你。 而且这不是一个特工；而是一名特工。你想要多少就多少。 你可以在早餐时启动五个，在上下班时让它们运行，从火车上登记，杀死你不喜欢的，从站台上启动另外三个，当你到达办公桌时，你已经拥有了一支小型私人劳动力在你的指挥下。 他们为你工作，或者看起来是这样。

到一月份，元超级智能实验室联合负责人纳特·弗里德曼 (Nat Friedman) 决定让一名特工接管他的健康状况。 他把自己的血液测试结果、DNA 和家里的摄像头交给了它，并告诉它采取一切措施让他多喝水。 一天晚上，特工确定他脱水了。 “我可以在摄像机上看到你，”WhatsApp 给他发来的信息。 “我希望你现在就去厨房喝一瓶水，我会看着确保你这么做。” 他服从了。 它给他发了一张自己喝酒的照片，并说：“干得好。” 他承认，他觉得自己做得很好。 几天后，他开着自己的特斯拉自动驾驶汽车回家，与工作人员交换有关他睡眠的语音信息，工作人员建议补充镁补充剂。 他说他没有。 车子转向了。 “附近有一家全食超市，”经纪人说。 “我已经重定向了你的导航。” 他进去买了镁。

安德烈·卡帕蒂 (Andrej Karpathy) 是 OpenAI 联合创始人，曾任特斯拉人工智能负责人，他自己编写代码已经有 20 年了。 他是其他程序员研究的那​​种程序员。 在 Opus 4.5 发布后的几周内，他就停止了。 特工们建造了他想要的任何东西。 他说，这是他二十年来工作中最大的改变。 自 12 月以来，他没有写过一行代码。 和弗里德曼一样，他也有一名代理人负责管理他的房子。 它叫多比。

工蚁并没有逃离他们的新霸主。 他们正在日以继夜地建造它。 在最大的实验室 Anthropic 和 OpenAI 中，研究人员每天工作 16 个小时，让智能体摆脱过去需要一周时间才能解决的问题，并利用节省下来的时间让更多智能体解决更多问题。 目前，这些模型仍然需要人类来训练它们。 消除人力是每个实验室的首要任务。 他们竞相让自己失业。 他们期望成功。 他们说，编码将在六个月内得到解决。 他们自己的大部分工作将在 18 年内实现自动化。 “硅谷现在充满了狂热的能量，”硅谷最著名的投资者之一埃拉德·吉尔告诉我。 “过去六个月发生了非常大的转变。”

您可能会想，这一切都与您无关。 你不写代码。 您不经营实验室。 你的工作涉及人、纸张或你手中可以握着的东西。 那么，考虑一下我。 J。 古德写于 1965 年。 古德是一位在战争期间帮助破译德国密码的英国数学家，他想象出一台足够聪明的机器，可以设计出比自己更好的机器。 他观察到，这样的机器将是“人类需要做出的最后一项发明”。 几十年后，科幻小说作家弗诺·文奇给这个预言起了一个名字：奇点。 它描述了机器不再需要人类不断变得更聪明的那一刻，之后人类历史的进程将变得对人类来说是不可知的。

在硅谷，问题不再是它是否会到来，而是它是否已经到来。 支付公司 Stripe 的联合创始人帕特里克·科里森 (Patrick Collison) 以倒数天数的方式拉开了年度会议的序幕。 “今天是 4 月 29 日，”他告诉人群，“当然，也被称为奇点的第 119 天。” 第一天是 2026 年 1 月 1 日。 他说，他是在开玩笑。 但只有一点点。

第二天，在同一个舞台上，弗里德曼告诉科里森，这是奇点的缓慢部分。 科里森问剩下的事情会有多奇怪。 “很奇怪，”弗里德曼说。 “未来几年我们可能会处于永久的震惊状态。”

末日对于商业来说是极好的。 投资者对经纪人感到愤怒，因为他们除了其他一切之外，还为了赚钱。 Anthropic 于 2023 年 3 月实现了第一笔收入，年初的收入就达到了 90 亿美元。 五个月后，这个数字达到了 470 亿美元。 2026 年前三个月，风险投资家向初创企业投入了 3000 亿美元，是之前记录的两倍多。 SpaceX 于 6 月上市，市值 1.75 万亿美元。 Anthropic 和 OpenAI 正在竞相跟进，这可能是有史以来规模最大的三笔股票发行。 市场已经接近历史高位。 每个人都在变得富有。

靠近这一刻的中心的是一位 37 岁的女性，身高略超过 5 英尺，一头金发，精力充沛，超出了她的体格所能承受的范围。 当她说话时，她的整个身体都陷入了思绪的上升气流中。 她的名字叫萨拉·郭。 她是一名科技投资者。 直到 2022 年，她一直是硅谷最古老的风险投资公司之一 Greylock Partners 历史上最年轻的普通合伙人。 然后她离开并创办了自己的基金，正式命名为Conviction。 她建立了一个唯一的前提：人工智能将像工业革命一样伟大。 她的前两个电话分别是 OpenAI 联合创始人萨姆·奥尔特曼 (Sam Altman) 和纳特·弗里德曼 (Nat Friedman)。

在 ChatGPT 出现之前，在全世界有理由相信人工智能即将成为某种特殊事物之前，郭已经向 Baseten 和 Harvey 写入了种子支票。 目前每家公司的估值均超过 110 亿美元。 她对它们的投资增加了一百多倍。 在 Conviction 的第一年，她对 Sierra、Cognition 和 Mistral 进行了早期检查；这三家公司现在的总价值达 540 亿美元。 迄今为止，在 21 家估值超过 100 亿美元、营收超过 1 亿美元的本土人工智能公司中，Conviction 已经支持了 6 家。

她在 Conviction 的合伙人是 Facebook 前高管兼红杉资本合伙人 Mike Vernal；他的妻子是 Anthropic 的首席产品官。 安德烈·卡帕蒂 (Andrej Karpathy) 在五月份加入 Anthropic 之前，曾在 Conviction 的办公室工作。 十多年来，郭一直与英伟达创始人黄仁勋关系密切。 她是许多最重要的工蚁的朋友。

换句话说，她可能会分担一般的发烧。 她没有。

“这当然可能是因为我没有给予足够的重视，”郭告诉我。 “但我觉得与六个月或一年前相比，疯狂的能量没有阶跃函数变化。”

相反，她全神贯注于一个两年前听起来很荒谬的问题。 不是特工们是否会很快统治地球，而是考虑到自我改进机器的巨大阴影，是否还有公司可以建造或投资。 它的创造者不再满足于出售该模型。 他们还打算在此基础上构建一切，包括工具、代理和应用程序，填补新公司可能建立的每一个角落和缝隙。 市场正在付出代价，就好像他们可能会成功一样。 今年第一季度（行业历史上最大的一个季度）部署的 3000 亿美元风险投资中，每美元中有 65 美分流向了四家现有公司：Anthropic、OpenAI、xAI 和 Waymo。

“我想要的未来，”郭告诉我，“不是一家拥有全能模式的公司，它对社会的消耗速度快得我们不知道该怎么办。” 这种感觉越来越普遍。 这些实验室今年提高了代币的价格，在某些情况下提高了一百倍，而他们的客户已经开始反抗。 他们不想建立在另一家公司的模式之上——付钱给它，给它提供数据，训练它，实际上是为了有一天构建他们已经构建的东西。 Palantir 首席执行官亚历克斯·卡普 (Alex Karp) 在接受 CNBC 采访时表示，他的企业客户非常愤怒。 “夹具已经拉好了，”他说。 郭自己的投资组合中的一位创始人说得更直白。 他不想一辈子都喝 Anthropic 和 OpenAI 的水。

郭已经成为叛乱事实上的领导人。 从某种意义上说，她别无选择。 当公司还只是一个想法时，信念就会支持它们，然后随着它们的成长不断投资。 她对那些一旦汇款就“消失在远方”的种子投资者没有耐心。 第一期基金规模为1亿美元。 现在有三笔，总额近十亿美元，其中一些支票投入到了已经过了创意阶段的公司。 但当公司成立时，实验室已经太大了。 “从 2023 年到 2026 年，你不是 Anthropic 或 OpenAI 的早期投资者，”她告诉我。 “就这么简单。”

不那么简单的是她所处的位置。 她的赌注是实验室无法建造一切。 但她所押注的公司每家的价值都接近一万亿美元，雇佣了几位亲密的朋友，并且正在夜以继日地致力于改进机器的自我改进，据他们自己承认，之后没有人知道会发生什么。 与之相对的是约克街的一家八人公司，中间有一个上拉杆。 这不是一个公平的竞争环境。 就连她自己的一些投资者今年也做出了同样的决定，并来找她说没有什么可以投资的了。 但没有一个站在郭的另一边的人会告诉你枪声已经平息了。

要进入郭的花园，你需要穿过一个棋盘。 这些方块被设置在车道和游泳池之间的小路上，每个方块都足够宽，可以站在上面，紫色的棋子与绿色的棋子相对排列，三月一个阳光明媚的星期六，我在棋子之间行走，发现郭在凉棚下，正在与贝拉·加西亚-卡马戈就创始人的问题进行深入的争论。

与郭发生争执是很正常的，Conviction 的投资者加西亚-卡马戈在接受这份工作之前就已经了解到这一点。 她曾在斯坦福大学划船并为美国国家队效力，然后在布里奇沃特度过了一段时光。 当 Conviction 打电话过来时，她正在权衡 OpenAI 提供的应用工程师职位。 加西亚-卡马戈记得，郭的建议并不是宣扬定罪，而是一次挑战。 “如果你想做别的事情，”郭告诉她，“就让它成为你可能做的最激进的事情。 我很高兴给凯文打电话，我们将为您找到一份更好的工作。 但那份[工作]对你来说还不够激进。” Kevin Weil 当时是 OpenAI 的首席产品官。

当郭和加西亚-卡马戈深陷其中时，他们背后的财产却充满了创始人。 总共 35 家，分布在 14 家公司。 定罪将他们从温哥华、特拉维夫、伦敦和塔林空运过来，然后将他们分配到旧金山各地的七个 Airbnb 房源中。 他们先后通过了OpenAI、Scale、Ramp、Kalshi、MIT、Anduril；其中一位曾担任肯·格里芬的幕僚长。 最小的孩子前一天刚满 18 岁。 在高中辍学之前，他已跻身爱沙尼亚前五名程序员之列。 他的雇主预计他今年会在克劳德身上花费 210 万美元。 他们送给他一个更快的型号 Opus 4.6 作为他的生日礼物。

从泳池边来看，这一切都看不出来，那里的场景看起来就像是 WeWork 夏令营。 网球场上有几个投篮。 其他人则乘坐滑索穿过树林。 有些人陷进了瀑布边的沙发里。 在花园的远处边缘，这位 18 岁的年轻人戴着一顶大了几号的黑色棒球帽——正面的小写字母写着“特工”——在蹦床上跳了起来。 其余的人坐在郭文贵身边，坐在凉棚下的两张长桌旁，低着头，低着 MacBook，开发软件来拨打外线销售电话、进行市场研究、拍摄电影、设计虚拟世界、管理宠物的礼宾需求，以及发动“代理战”。

这就是 Embed，一个加速器（尽管不叫它加速器），Conviction 自 2023 年以来每年运行两次。 他们从大约 1000 份申请中选择了大约 12 个团队，开出一张 25 万美元的小额支票，并将它们视为投资组合公司 10 周。 每个周期都以一个周末开始，就像我亲眼所见的那样，创始人们被引入了信念之路。 那个周末的演讲者包括 Bret Taylor、Mike Krieger、Parker Conrad 和 Andrej Karpathy。 该项目以在 100 名风险投资家面前进行的演示日结束。

“我讨厌加速器这个词，”郭在 Conviction 最早聘用的普拉纳夫·雷迪 (Pranav Reddy) 说。 “哪个非常聪明的 21 岁年轻人会对‘加速’感到兴奋？” Conviction 希望从 Embed 赚钱，但这并不是真正的重点。 郭对这项练习的解释很简单。 她一直想知道正在发生什么，而最可靠的了解方法就是会见推动技术前沿的最年轻的公司。 这也是每年两次的征税。 世界上最有前途的年轻创始人聚集在一起，坐在泰勒家族和卡帕蒂家族前面，然后被送回机器的阴影下进行建设。

她的一周是在不太田园风光的环境中开始的。 五天前，她在圣何塞主持了英伟达年度开发者大会上黄仁勋主题演讲的三小时赛前赛，她身穿淡紫色西装外套，翻领上别着一面小美国国旗，耳朵上戴着广播耳机。 Nvidia 是地球上最有价值的公司，价值 5 万亿美元。 它设计的芯片就是繁荣的组成部分。 大约 15,000 人已经鱼贯进入会议厅，看着郭，等待着黄。
